Version 1.14.0 adds covariate-dependent cohort-assignment DGPs (multinomial-logit ordered-logit) Monte Carlo studies can stress-test FETWFE realistic regime cohort selection depends observable characteristics, version 1.48.0 adds targeted-sparsity coefficient mode (n_signal_cohorts / treat_base_levels) controlled, non-degenerate high-dimensional data-generating processes. pipeline streamlines simulation experiments can rapidly evaluate FETWFE’s performance varying scenarios. details, consult package documentation reach author.

title: Simultaneous confidence bands and the zero-effect test
text: package’s per-point standard errors Wald intervals deliver (1 - alpha) coverage per effect: single cohort ATT, single event-time coefficient, CI contains true value probability approximately 1 - alpha. make claim involving multiple effects jointly — example, “event-time coefficient significantly positive 5% level” — per-effect coverage imply family-wise coverage. probability every coefficient family K effects lies per-point CI can far 1 - alpha K large. simultaneousCIs() returns intervals maintain family-wise coverage requested 1 - alpha, using exact joint multivariate-normal critical value c1−αc_{1-\alpha} defined (1−α)(1 - \alpha) quantile max⁡k|Zk|\max_k |Z_k| Z∼N(0,ρ)Z \sim N(0, \rho) ρ\rho correlation matrix effect family’s joint covariance. Theorem (c′') (paper_arxiv.tex:1233) Assumption (Ψ-)(\Psi\text{-}) (assumption equation paper_arxiv.tex:2013; -prose discussion implies tight Gaussianity paper line 1268), joint Gaussianity exact asymptotically; paper’s fixed-dim framing (AE 1(d)), high-dimensional correction needed. event-study family fitted res_default object setup : function returns simultaneous_cis S3 object $ci (interval data frame), $critical_value (simultaneous c1−αc_{1-\alpha}), $pointwise_critical_value (z1−α/2z_{1-\alpha/2}, reference), $bonferroni_critical_value (z1−α/(2K)z_{1-\alpha/(2K)}, conservative family-wise upper bound). effects positively correlated — typically , event-study coefficients share regression-coefficient variance piece — simultaneous critical value sits strictly pointwise Bonferroni values, giving tighter intervals Bonferroni preserving family-wise coverage. four available family options "event_study" (one effect per post-treatment event time), "cohort" (one effect per treated cohort), "all_post_treatment" (one effect per (g, t) cell), "custom" (user-supplied K x num_treats contrast matrix following multcomp::glht() convention; see ?simultaneousCIs). critical value computed via mvtnorm::qmvnorm(..., algorithm = mvtnorm::GenzBretz()) (mvtnorm’s default quasi-Monte Carlo integrator), sub-second typical fit realistic FETWFE family (K cap practical concern). function deterministic inputs: fit plus family plus alpha plus contrasts produces critical value across calls. achieved save/restoring caller’s .Random.seed using fixed internal seed immediately qmvnorm() call; function mutate caller’s RNG state. version 1.16.0 mvtnorm package Imports dependency (ships package), simultaneous bands now computed default fit time — see ci_type subsection . (versions 1.15.x mvtnorm Suggests loaded simultaneousCIs() called.) plot.simultaneous_cis method additionally requires ggplot2, Suggests; install install.packages("ggplot2") already. default analytic path matches ::aggte(cband = TRUE) Callaway-Sant’Anna package, computes simultaneous bands via CCK (2013) multiplier bootstrap. Asymptotically two critical values identical fixed-dim Gaussianity; analytic simultaneousCIs() path deterministic sub-second, whereas multiplier bootstrap stochastic resampling-based.
code: sci <- simultaneousCIs(res_default, family = "event_study", alpha = 0.05) print(sci) #> Parametric simultaneous (1 - alpha) confidence intervals #> Family: event_study, K = 5, alpha = 0.05 #> Simultaneous critical value: 2.3693 (pointwise 1.9600, Bonferroni 2.5758) #>  #>  effect   estimate simultaneous_ci_low simultaneous_ci_high pointwise_ci_low #>      e0  0.0000000           0.0000000            0.0000000        0.0000000 #>      e1 -0.6726640          -0.9531319           -0.3921962       -0.9046805 #>      e2 -0.6726640          -0.9531319           -0.3921962       -0.9046805 #>      e3 -3.0092312          -3.4694847           -2.5489777       -3.3899750 #>      e4  0.1684927          -0.4085822            0.7455677       -0.3088914 #>  pointwise_ci_high #>          0.0000000 #>         -0.4406476 #>         -0.4406476 #>         -2.6284874 #>          0.6458769

title: Simultaneous confidence bands and the zero-effect test
text: simultaneousCIs(..., method = "bootstrap") computes sup-t critical value CCK (2013) multiplier bootstrap directly — procedure uses. perturbs per-unit influence functions f̂(k)\hat f_i^{(k)} random multipliers ξi\xi_i (multiplier = "rademacher" "mammen"), forms T(b)=max⁡k|1N∑iξif̂(k)|/sd̂kT^{(b)} = \max_k |\frac{1}{N}\sum_i \xi_i \hat f_i^{(k)}| / \widehat{\mathrm{sd}}_k BB replicates, takes (1−α)(1-\alpha) quantile. analytic bootstrap critical values agree Monte-Carlo error, sit pointwise Bonferroni values: Two reasons prefer bootstrap. First, scales large effect families: qmvnorm integration strains hundreds effects (e.g. simultaneous bands full disaggregated cohort ×\times event-time grid), whereas bootstrap stays stable linear B⋅KB \cdot K. Second, heteroskedasticity/cluster-robust construction — per-unit influence matrix FF reproduces cluster-robust joint covariance (crossprod(F)/(NT)2\mathrm{crossprod}(F)/(NT)^2 equals N/(N−1)N/(N-1) finite-sample factor), se_type = "cluster" fit bootstrap per-effect standard errors equal analytic ones exactly (default fit bootstrap reports cluster-robust band regardless). Pass integer seed reproducible draws; seed = NULL draws come ambient RNG. method = "bootstrap" supports four families. family = "event_study" bootstrap additionally perturbs second, independent multiplier stream per-unit cohort-probability (propensity) influence function, alongside regression-channel stream — two-channel bootstrap reproducing analytic variance decomposition Σ=Σ1+Σ2\Sigma = \Sigma_1 + \Sigma_2. event-study family one whose propensity term Σ2\Sigma_2 non-zero, event-time effect pools across cohorts treated event time, weighted estimated cohort probabilities π̂g=Ng/N\hat\pi_g = N_g / N; per-unit propensity influence function cohort-sample-proportion summand ξi=eWi−π̂\xi_i = e_{W_i} - \hat\pi (paper assumption (Ψ-)(\Psi\text{-})). two channels asymptotically uncorrelated (paper Theorem C.1, Step 5), variances add cross-term — exactly multipliers drawn independently. se_type = "cluster" fit event-study bootstrap per-effect standard errors equal analytic ones machine precision, critical value matches analytic qmvnorm one Monte-Carlo error: High-dimensional p≥NTp \geq NT. (full) design high-dimensional — analytic Gram inverse need exist, analytic sup-t band — bootstrap automatically switches full-design desparsified construction debiasedATT() (high-dimensional FETWFE theory): effect’s debiasing direction becomes nodewise (riesz_lasso) relaxed inverse singular Gram, bridge residuals. uniformly valid (post-selection) route simultaneous bands regime. validated simulation (fetwfe() fits ): scalar overall-ATT coverage (Theorem debiased.highdim.thm) family-wise band coverage (Theorem debiased.highdim.joint.thm, ≈0.92 event-study family p>NTp > NT anchor Faletto 2025) near-nominal, returned object carries regime field per-effect feasibility / converged / lambda_node diagnostics, lambda_c tunes nodewise penalty lambda_node = lambda_c * max(||) * sqrt(log(p) / N) — -small values leave directions infeasible trigger warning. lambda_c accepts either fixed positive number (default theory scale 1.0) string "cv", selects constant per fit cross-validating Riesz loss KKT-feasible region (desparsified-lasso / auto-DML standard), falling back 1.0 grid penalty feasible; one constant serves debiasedATT() point estimate every bootstrap band effect (issue #295). default stays fixed 1.0; overall-ATT coverage validated Faletto (2025) robust across penalty values studied (CV selector theory scale fixed constant , bracketing default 1.0). simulator can generate p > NT panels needs (see simulateData() documentation). family = "event_study" uses desparsified path high-dimensional regime, additionally carrying per-unit propensity channel (two-channel Σ1+Σ2\Sigma_1 + \Sigma_2 bootstrap). high-dimensional regime band centered debiased estimate (high-dimensional FETWFE theory correction, matching debiasedATT()) rather post-selection bridge estimate, otherwise carry selection bias. desparsified path fetwfe()-; non-fetwfe() p≥NTp \geq NT fit (e.g. betwfe()) debiased band, falls back fixed-pp selected-support band (rather erroring) emits warning(). coverage study (issue #308) shows fallback band substantially -covers p≥NTp \geq NT regime — even selected support low-dimensional — bridge shrinks band center toward zero (biased away truth) post-selection standard error understates sampling variability; treat band unreliable use fetwfe() fit valid high-dimensional simultaneous bands. See vignette("high_dimensional_vignette") debiasedATT() accompanying high-dimensional diagnostics.
code: sci_boot <- simultaneousCIs(res_default, family = "all_post_treatment",                             method = "bootstrap", B = 1000, seed = 1) c(pointwise  = sci_boot$pointwise_critical_value,   analytic   = simultaneousCIs(res_default, family = "all_post_treatment")$critical_value,   bootstrap  = sci_boot$critical_value,   bonferroni = sci_boot$bonferroni_critical_value) #>  pointwise   analytic  bootstrap bonferroni  #>   1.959964   2.479436   2.374132   2.865260 es_boot <- simultaneousCIs(res_default, family = "event_study",                            method = "bootstrap", B = 1000, seed = 1) c(pointwise  = es_boot$pointwise_critical_value,   analytic   = simultaneousCIs(res_default, family = "event_study")$critical_value,   bootstrap  = es_boot$critical_value,   bonferroni = es_boot$bonferroni_critical_value) #>  pointwise   analytic  bootstrap bonferroni  #>   1.959964   2.369258   2.355859   2.575829

what: Simultaneous bands are the default: the ci_type argument
title: Simultaneous confidence bands and the zero-effect test
text: version 1.16.0, confidence-interval bounds fetwfe() / etwfe() / betwfe() / twfeCovs() return display simultaneous default — matching ’s cband = TRUE default. new fit-time argument ci_type = c("simultaneous", "pointwise") controls : ci_type = "simultaneous" (default) makes returned catt_df$ci_low / catt_df$ci_high cohort-family simultaneous bounds, eventStudy()’s ci_low / ci_high event-study-family simultaneous bounds — bounds simultaneousCIs(fit, family = "cohort") / simultaneousCIs(fit, family = "event_study") return. print(), summary(), plot() display (labeled [simultaneous 95% CI]), broom::tidy() (fit, eventStudy(), cohortStudy()) surfaces . ci_type = "pointwise" restores pre-1.16.0 per-effect Wald intervals everywhere (bounds get estimate ± qnorm(1 - alpha/2) * se). interval bounds per-cohort p-values (p_value) follow ci_type. "simultaneous" p_value single-step max-T multiplicity-adjusted (family-wise) p-value matching band; "pointwise" per-effect Wald p-value. two correspond exactly: coefficient lies outside 1 - alpha simultaneous band iff adjusted p_value < alpha (version 1.18.0, #200). standard errors (se), selection flags (selected), overall-ATT confidence interval p-value (single scalar, K = 1, family hence widening) identical settings — bounds difference critical-value multiplier (qnorm(1 - alpha/2) pointwise vs joint c1−αc_{1-\alpha} simultaneous). tidiers pass object’s bounds straight , every CI surface package agrees default: broom::tidy(fit)’s cohort rows, summary(fit), cohortStudy(fit), plot(fit, type = "catt") report simultaneous bounds. (overall-ATT row broom::tidy(fit) stays scalar pointwise interval.) standard errors unavailable (q >= 1 bridge estimators, rank-deficient design) bounds NA settings, fit still succeeds — simultaneous path degrades cleanly (NA) pointwise bounds rather throwing error.
code: fit_sim <- fetwfeWithSimulatedData(sim_data) # ci_type = "simultaneous" (default) fit_pw <- fetwfeWithSimulatedData(sim_data, ci_type = "pointwise")  # Same point estimates and SEs; only the bounds differ (simultaneous wider). cbind(   estimate = fit_sim$catt_df$estimate,   se = fit_sim$catt_df$se,   simul_width = fit_sim$catt_df$ci_high - fit_sim$catt_df$ci_low,   pointwise_width = fit_pw$catt_df$ci_high - fit_pw$catt_df$ci_low ) #>        estimate         se simul_width pointwise_width #> [1,] -0.4137526 0.07754859   0.3463267       0.3039849 #> [2,] -1.6817852 0.12781952   0.5708332       0.5010433 #> [3,]  0.0000000 0.00000000   0.0000000       0.0000000

what: 2. Selection consistency as an implicit zero-effect test
title: Simultaneous confidence bands and the zero-effect test
text: question applied users ask constantly : cohort’s treatment effect distinguishable zero — can conclude actually zero? fetwfe() (betwfe()), answer built estimator. bridge penalty’s restriction selection consistency (Faletto 2025, Theorem 6.2) makes selected flag catt_df implicit hypothesis test H0:τATT(g)=0H_0: \tau_{\text{ATT}}(g) = 0, free, extra computation. mapping : selected = TRUE (estimate ≠0\neq 0) asymptotic analogue reject H0H_0: effect nonzero. selected = FALSE (estimate exactly 00) asymptotic analogue fail reject H0H_0 — , Theorem 6.2, stronger statement truth zero probability tending one. conclusion selected-cohort delivered selected flag, p-value. cohort penalty zeros estimate == 0, se == 0, p_value == NA construction — Wald p-value read, inferential content lives entirely selected. (condensed, first-time-user-oriented version discussion appears “Testing zero-effect null” main package vignette; treatment deeper, inference-focused one.)

previous_headings: 2. Selection consistency as an implicit zero-effect test
what: Why this is stronger than a confidence interval on an unregularized estimator
title: Simultaneous confidence bands and the zero-effect test
text: unregularized estimator — OLS, plain etwfe() — coefficient estimate β̂\hat\beta continuous random variable, β̂=0\hat\beta = 0exactly measure-zero event: essentially never happens, carry information truth . zero-effect inference estimator offers “confidence interval contains 00,” .e. fail reject — never accept. Selection consistency changes picture qualitatively. Theorem 6.2, β̂j=0\hat\beta_j = 0 exactly probability →1\1 truth zero, β̂j≠0\hat\beta_j \neq 0 probability →1\1 truth nonzero, observing β̂j=0\hat\beta_j = 0is informative. paper states consequence directly: τATT(g,t)=0\tau_{\text{ATT}}(g,t) = 0 lim⁡N→∞P(τ̂ATT(g,t)=0)=1\lim_{N\\infty} P(\hat\tau_{\text{ATT}}(g,t) = 0) = 1, calls “stronger convergence probability 00” (Faletto 2025, around statement Theorem 6.2). worth precise part theory delivers test, paper careful . conventional Wald statistic test zero null setting: relevant effect truly zero statistic asymptotically normal (converges probability 00 even scaling N\sqrt{N}), valid Wald test H0:τ=0H_0: \tau = 0 construct. precisely right tool selection consistency selected flag, rather Wald statistic point estimate. complementary half theory backs effects estimator retains: effect truly nonzero, FETWFE N\sqrt{N}-consistent asymptotically Gaussian selected support (oracle property, Theorem 6.3), practical Wald confidence intervals p-values Theorem 6.4 — machinery documented vignette("inference_vignette"). selected = TRUE cohorts come usual valid CIs finite p-values, selected = FALSE cohorts carry stronger, selection-based zero-effect conclusion.

title: Simultaneous confidence bands and the zero-effect test
text: interpretation rests selection consistency, paper proves bridge exponent q∈(0,1)q \(0, 1). package default q=0.5q = 0.5 satisfies . q=1q = 1 (lasso) selection consistency FETWFE proven, implicit-test framing apply; q=2q = 2 (ridge) solver never zeros coefficients , selected trivially TRUE everywhere conveys nothing. Read selected flag zero-effect test default (q<1q < 1) bridge penalty.

previous_headings: 2. Selection consistency as an implicit zero-effect test
title: Simultaneous confidence bands and the zero-effect test
text: cleanest way see implicit test simulated data truth known. draw coefficients middle treated cohort true average treatment effect exactly zero two neighbors nonzero, check whether FETWFE selects exactly zero cohort. getTes() reports known per-cohort truth comparison. true effects (−1.6,0,1.333)(-1.6, 0, 1.333): cohort 3’s effect exactly zero. FETWFE selects precisely cohort — cohort 3 returns selected = FALSE estimate exactly 00 p_value = NA — recovers two truly-nonzero cohorts selected = TRUE finite standard errors p-values. Reading selected column test mapping : cohort 3 “fail reject (conclude zero),” cohorts 2 4 “reject.” estimator performed zero-effect test automatically, verdict matches known truth.
code: # Known truth: cohort 3 (the middle treated cohort) has a true effect of # exactly zero; cohorts 2 and 4 are nonzero. coefs <- genCoefs(G = 3, T = 6, d = 2, density = 0.2, eff_size = 2, seed = 3) getTes(coefs)$actual_cohort_tes #> [1] -1.600000  0.000000  1.333333  dat <- simulateData(coefs, N = 120, sig_eps_sq = 1, sig_eps_c_sq = 0.5, seed = 3) res <- fetwfeWithSimulatedData(dat, verbose = FALSE)  # The implicit test: the `selected` and `p_value` columns of catt_df. res$catt_df[, c("cohort", "estimate", "se", "p_value", "selected")] #>   cohort  estimate         se p_value selected #> 1      2 -1.425100 0.12554417       0     TRUE #> 2      3  0.000000 0.00000000      NA    FALSE #> 3      4  1.426826 0.09635078       0     TRUE

what: Scope: this is the post-treatment zero-effect test
title: Simultaneous confidence bands and the zero-effect test
text: package currently estimates post-treatment treatment effects (event times e≥0e \geq 0), demonstration specifically post-treatment zero-effect test, surfaced catt_df. selection-consistency logic support two applications corresponding extended regression specifications exist: () selecting pre-treatment placebo coefficients give parallel-trends test, (b) selecting heterogeneous-trend augmenting parameters give homogeneous-trends test. package estimate terms today, vignette makes claim perform either test; noted natural extensions selected--implicit-test reasoning cover specifications land.

title: Fused Extended Two-Way Fixed Effects
text: fetwfe package implements fused extended two-way fixed effects (FETWFE), methodology estimating treatment effects difference--differences staggered adoptions. staggered-adoption settings — units become treated different times — treatment effects typically vary across adoption cohorts time since treatment. conventional two-way fixed effects (TWFE) estimator biased effects heterogeneous like , effectively uses already-treated units controls newly-treated ones, contaminating estimate. FETWFE instead fits fully heterogeneous model — separate effect every cohort time period — applies fusion penalty shrinks together effects similar across neighboring cohorts periods. pools cohort--time effects data-driven way (collapsing genuinely agree, keeping distinct differ) , unlike ad hoc model selection, comes asymptotically valid standard errors confidence intervals resulting estimates. brief introduction methodology, well background difference--differences staggered adoptions motivation FETWFE, see blog post. detailed slides methodology (less detailed paper), see . Check recent draft full paper . blog post explains little package hood, ’re interested.

what: A worked example: fit, extract, plot
title: Fused Extended Two-Way Fixed Effects
text: primary function fetwfe(). applied divorce dataset bacondecomp package — unilateral (“-fault”) divorce / female-suicide panel Stevenson Wolfers (2006), reproducing empirical application Faletto (2025, Sec. 8.2): 1. overall ATT. aggregated effect 95% Wald interval: (summary(res) prints full report — overall ATT, per-cohort event-study effects, model details.) 2. heterogeneity underneath. overall ATT average; cohortStudy() eventStudy() return effects adoption cohort time since treatment tidy data frames — effects FETWFE kept distinct rather fusing zero: 3. Plot . plot() draws event-study estimates confidence intervals (per-cohort average effects type = "catt"):  full set vignettes function documentation, see package website CRAN page.
code: library(fetwfe) library(bacondecomp)  data(divorce) # Female subset; `changed` is the absorbing 0/1 divorce-reform indicator, and the # response is the elasticity-scaled female suicide rate. Noise variances are # supplied (precomputed by REML) to keep the call fast. divorce_f <- divorce[divorce$sex == 2, ]  res <- fetwfe(     pdata = divorce_f,     time_var = "year",     unit_var = "st",     treatment = "changed",     covs = c("murderrate", "lnpersinc", "afdcrolls"),     response = "suiciderate_elast_jag",     sig_eps_sq = 0.0344,     sig_eps_c_sq = 0.1507,     add_ridge = TRUE,     q = 0.5 ) round(c(     ATT = res$att_hat,     SE = res$att_se,     CI_low = res$att_hat - qnorm(0.975) * res$att_se,     CI_high = res$att_hat + qnorm(0.975) * res$att_se ), 4) #>     ATT      SE  CI_low CI_high  #> -0.0602  0.0188 -0.0970 -0.0233 cohortStudy(res) #>    cohort    estimate          se       ci_low      ci_high      p_value selected #> 1    1969  0.00000000 0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000           NA    FALSE #> 2    1970 -0.44401171 0.046498180 -0.572555500 -0.315467910 0.000000e+00     TRUE #> 3    1971 -0.02633974 0.020112012 -0.081939211  0.029259735 8.474215e-01     TRUE #> 4    1972 -0.01611957 0.009359074 -0.041992646  0.009753503 5.468636e-01     TRUE #> 5    1973 -0.06452464 0.013062067 -0.100634602 -0.028414670 7.037396e-06     TRUE #> 6    1974 -0.03001991 0.012978739 -0.065899516  0.005859696 1.707508e-01     TRUE #> 7    1975  0.00000000 0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000           NA    FALSE #> 8    1976 -0.04379642 0.063672429 -0.219818269  0.132225428 9.976219e-01     TRUE #> 9    1977 -0.12389080 0.024178412 -0.190731798 -0.057049799 2.691903e-06     TRUE #> 10   1980 -0.04013225 0.061547270 -0.210279123  0.130014613 9.984244e-01     TRUE #> 11   1984  0.00000000 0.000000000  0.000000000  0.000000000           NA    FALSE #> 12   1985  0.14972561 0.050875421  0.009080968  0.290370244 2.880518e-02     TRUE plot(res, type = "event_study")

title: Fused Extended Two-Way Fixed Effects
text: core differentiator FETWFE geometry fusion penalty, chosen via fusion_structure argument fetwfe(): "cohort" (default) fuses treatment effects within cohorts — two-way fusion structure Faletto (2025). "event_study" instead fuses effects time since treatment (event time e = t - g) across cohorts — package realization paper’s event-study-penalty theory. full control, fusion_matrix argument accepts invertible num_treats x num_treats differencing matrix D_N, encoding arbitrary fusion structure beyond two built-ins. fusion_structure option available genCoefs() simulation studies. guidance use, see vignette “Choosing fusion structure: cohort vs. event-study penalties” — vignette("fusion_structure_vignette", package = "fetwfe").

title: Assessment: #142 (multiplier-bootstrap simultaneous CIs) — 2026-06-15
text: package already ships exact, principled simultaneous (sup-t) bands via analytic simultaneousCIs() (R/simultaneous_cis.R, ~1088 lines): critical value (1 - alpha) quantile max_k |Z_k|, Z ~ N(0, cov2cor(Sigma)), computed deterministically mvtnorm::qmvnorm(). Families (event_study, cohort, all_post_treatment, custom), single-step max-T adjusted p-values, Bonferroni comparison already . multiplier bootstrap #142 specifies asymptotically equivalent bands. genuine, non-redundant value narrower issue implies (see “bootstrap actually differs”). foundation #142 names package: assumes “unit-level influence functions (IFs) first-class objects, bootstrap nothing perturb.” simultaneousCIs() builds joint covariance Sigma analytically (Gram-inverse sandwich + multinomial delta-method), per-unit IFs. per-unit matrix bootstrap resamples built first. Net: #142 moderate build mostly-redundant output. ’s worth specific reasons (); don’t weigh heavily, higher-leverage issue better use slot.

title: Assessment: #142 (multiplier-bootstrap simultaneous CIs) — 2026-06-15
text: Chernozhukov–Chetverikov–Kato (2013) multiplier bootstrap family treatment-effect estimates: Per-effect per-unit summand f_i^{(k)} effect k family. Multiplier weights xi_i^{(b)} (Mammen / Rademacher), b = 1..B. Sup-t statistic T^{(b)} = max_k |(1/sqrt N) sum_i xi_i^{(b)} f_i^{(k)}| / sqrt(v_N^{(k)}). Critical value c_hat = quantile_{1-alpha}({T^{(b)}}). Band: theta_hat_k +/- c_hat * sqrt(v_N^{(k)} / (NT)). Plus tests (coverage; c_hat pointwise ~1.96 Bonferroni) inference_vignette.Rmd section.

what: Key finding: the analytic machinery is not IF-based
title: Assessment: #142 (multiplier-bootstrap simultaneous CIs) — 2026-06-15
text: simultaneousCIs() assembles Sigma = Sigma_1 + Sigma_2: Sigma_1 (regression channel) — .assemble_joint_cov_var1() Psi (p_sel x K) Gram inverse getGramInv() (R/simultaneous_cis.R:445-498). closed-form sandwich Psi' G_inv Psi * sig_eps_sq, average per-unit terms. Sigma_2 (cohort-probability channel) — .assemble_joint_cov_var2() multinomial covariance cohort proportions per-effect Jacobians (R/simultaneous_cis.R:499-525). N_units x K per-unit matrix anywhere path. issue’s stated prerequisite (“without unit-level IFs first-class objects, bootstrap nothing perturb”) literally unmet. “Gaussianity upgrade” issue depends (#141, tight Gaussian variance, landed 1.12.0) delivered hat_v_N / tilde_v_N variance components — per-unit IFs.

what: Why the bootstrap is (mostly) redundant here
title: Assessment: #142 (multiplier-bootstrap simultaneous CIs) — 2026-06-15
text: multiplier bootstrap Rademacher/Mammen weights, B -> infinity, reproduces max_k |Z_k| Z ~ N(0, Sigma_emp), Sigma_emp = (1/N) sum_i f_i f_i' empirical covariance. object qmvnorm() already integrates analytically. paper’s fixed-dim tight-Gaussianity (Theorem (c’)), MVN law exact asymptotic law, : analytic bands principled answer deterministic (Monte-Carlo noise, seed). bootstrap band , best, noisier estimate quantity.

what: Where the bootstrap actually differs (the honest case for it)
title: Assessment: #142 (multiplier-bootstrap simultaneous CIs) — 2026-06-15
text: Three real, non-redundant reasons — worth weighing: Heteroskedasticity / cluster robustness free. default analytic Sigma_1 uses homoskedastic sig_eps_sq * Psi' G_inv Psi. bootstrap’s Sigma_emp uses actual per-unit residual products, naturally cluster/heteroskedasticity- robust without user choosing se_type = "cluster". (Caveat: package already offers cluster-robust sandwich, overlaps existing functionality.) Scaling large families. qmvnorm() integration gets slow / less accurate K grows (dozens event-study × cohort effects). bootstrap scales linearly B*K stays stable. defensible advantage. -parity / reviewer expectation. ::aggte(..., cband = TRUE) bootstrap; JoE reviewer asked simultaneous bands. Shipping bootstrap labelled like competition communication value even analytic band good better. None statistical necessity given analytic bands; robustness / scaling / familiarity arguments.

title: Assessment: #142 (multiplier-bootstrap simultaneous CIs) — 2026-06-15
text: per-unit matrix assemblable pieces already exist: Regression channel. psi_k' tau_hat f_i^{reg,k} = psi_k' A_sel G_inv X_i' eps_i (unit ’s rows selected support, times residuals). debiasedATT() already computes exactly one direction: score <- (X %*% v) * resid; unit_scores <- tapply(score, unit, sum) (R/debiased_att.R). Generalizing v (vector) K-column direction matrix D = (Psi' A_sel G_inv)' yields N_units x K regression-matrix directly. Propensity channel. Sigma_2 already uses per-cohort multinomial Jacobians; per-unit propensity J_k' (e_{cohort_i} - pi), assemblable Jacobians unit’s cohort membership. : build F = F_reg + F_pi (N_units x K), bootstrap loop ~20 lines. Estimate: focused days incl. tests + vignette — multi-week effort, real, adds second, Monte-Carlo, seed-dependent inference path maintain alongside analytic one.

title: Assessment: #142 (multiplier-bootstrap simultaneous CIs) — 2026-06-15
text: Full #142 — build matrix + faithful CCK bootstrap. work; exactly issue specifies; delivers heteroskedasticity-robust + large-K-scalable bootstrap -parity. Adds maintained Monte-Carlo path. Lighter — Gaussian multiplier existing analytic Sigma. Draw Z^{(b)} ~ N(0, Sigma) take sup-t quantile. Cheap (reuses Sigma), gives cband-style B-replication API matching analytic bands expectation — per-unit-bootstrap issue specifies (heteroskedasticity robustness, large-K speed win; ’s just Monte-Carlo qmvnorm exactly). Mostly cosmetic / API-parity move. Hard justify existing deterministic bands. Defer #142; take higher-leverage issue. analytic simultaneousCIs() already covers core need exact, deterministic band. Spend slot e.g. #5 (unbalanced panels — top applied-user blocker), #30 (pre-trends diagnostic), #33 (predict.fetwfe, already pushed branch). Optionally annotate #142 assessment leave open “nice--, higher-leverage work.”

title: #142 design + the high-dimensional scope decision — 2026-06-15
text: bootstrap perturbs per-unit influence-function matrix F (N_units x K, one column per effect family). F = F_reg + F_pi. bridge (reused, already exists). simultaneousCIs() builds, family, psi_tes_mat (K x num_treats, via .build_psi_tes_for_family(), R/simultaneous_cis.R:846–924) Psi <- t(psi_tes_mat %*% d_inv_treat_sel) (p_sel x K, R/simultaneous_cis.R:486) — column k Psi effect k’s direction selected coefficient space. input construction needs. Regression channel F_reg (generalizes debiasedATT). effect k: - debiasing direction v_k: - fixed-p: v_k <- solve(Sig + ridge, Psi[, k]) (R/debiased_att.R:356 pattern); - high-dim: v_k <- riesz_lasso(Sig, Psi[, k], lambda_node_k) (R/riesz_lasso.R:31, reuse #31). - per-row score score_k <- (X %*% v_k) * resid per-unit F_reg[, k] <- tapply(score_k, unit, sum) — exactly debiasedATT’s score/unit_scores lines (R/debiased_att.R:408–413), now K columns. - Shared across k: X, resid, Sig, unit index. Per-effect: Psi[,k], v_k, lambda_node_k. - Cost: K linear solves (fixed-p) K riesz_lasso coordinate-descent calls (high-dim, O(K · max_iter · p^2)), Sig formed . Propensity channel F_pi (one NEW derivation). package aggregate Sigma_2 (theta' J_k' Sigma_pi_hat J_l theta, R/variance_machinery.R:1259–1284), per-unit summand. cohort-probability estimator pi_hat_g = (1/N) sum_i 1{c_i = g}, per-unit (e_{c_i} - pi_bar) (e unit indicator, pi_bar cohort-prob vector), whose empirical covariance exactly Sigma_pi_hat = .multinomial_cov(...). Effect k’s propensity F_pi[, k] = (J_k %*% theta_sel)' (e_{c_i} - pi_bar), J_k per-effect Jacobian already built .build_j_list_for_family() (R/simultaneous_cis.R:942–980). event_study Sigma_2 != 0; cohort / all_post_treatment / custom give J_k = 0, F_pi = 0 . bootstrap. Draw xi_i^{(b)} (Rademacher / Mammen two-point), b = 1..B; T^{(b)} = max_k |sum_i xi_i^{(b)} F[,k]| / sqrt(N * v_N^{(k)}); critical value c_hat = quantile_{1-alpha}({T^{(b)}}); band estimate_k +/- c_hat * sqrt(v_N^{(k)} / (NT)), v_N^{(k)} = N * diag(Sigma)_k (N * crossprod(F)[k,k]/N).

text: attgtToFetwfeDf() reshapes renames panel dataset already formatted ::att_gt() (Callaway Sant'Anna 2021) can passed directly fetwfe() etwfe() fetwfe package. particular, creates absorbing-state treatment dummy equals 1 first treated period onward* 0 otherwise, (optionally) drops units already treated first period sample (fetwfe() removes internally), returns tidy dataframe whose column names match arguments fetwfe()/etwfe() expect.

title: Convert data formatted for att_gt() to a dataframe suitable for fetwfe() / etwfe() — attgtToFetwfeDf
code: attgtToFetwfeDf(   data,   yname,   tname,   idname,   gname,   covars = character(0),   drop_first_period_treated = TRUE,   out_names = list(time = "time_var", unit = "unit_var", treatment = "treatment",     response = "response"),   verbose = FALSE )

title: Convert data formatted for att_gt() to a dataframe suitable for fetwfe() / etwfe() — attgtToFetwfeDf
text: data data.frame long format containing least four columns used ::att_gt(): outcome yname, time tname, unit id idname, first-treatment period gname (0 never-treated group). yname Character scalar. Name outcome column. tname Character scalar. Name time variable (numeric integer). becomes time_var returned dataframe. idname Character scalar. Name unit identifier. Converted character returned unit_var. gname Character scalar. Name group variable holding first period treatment. Values must 0 never-treated, positive integer representing first treated period. covars Character vector additional covariate column names carry (default character(0)). columns left untouched appear required columns returned dataframe. drop_first_period_treated Logical. TRUE (default), units already treated first sample period removed creating treatment dummy. fetwfe() internally, dropping keeps returned dataframe cleaner. out_names named list giving column names use resulting dataframe. Defaults list(time = "time_var", unit = "unit_var", treatment = "treatment", response = "response"). Override prefer different names (instance, keep original yname). vector must contain exactly four names. verbose Logical. TRUE, message() reports count first-period-treated unit-period rows dropped drop_first_period_treated = TRUE. Default FALSE (silent).

title: Convert data formatted for att_gt() to a dataframe suitable for fetwfe() / etwfe() — attgtToFetwfeDf

what: Augment is not defined for a twfeCovs fit (documented omission) — augment.twfeCovs
title: Augment is not defined for a twfeCovs fit (documented omission) — augment.twfeCovs
text: augment() intentionally provided twfeCovs() objects (#58): fit's coefficient vector lives reduced cohort-level basis shared fitted-value path (X %*% beta_hat) match, meaningful .fitted / .resid reconstructed. Use tidy.twfeCovs(), glance.twfeCovs(), summary() instead. Calling method always raises error.

text: Implementation extended two-way fixed effects bridge penalty. Estimates overall ATT well CATT (cohort average treatment effects treated units).

text: pdata Dataframe; panel data set. row represent observation unit time. contain columns described . time_var Character; name single column containing variable time period. column expected contain integer values (example, years). Recommended encodings dates include format YYYY, YYYYMM, YYYYMMDD, whichever appropriate data. unit_var Character; name single column containing variable unit. column expected contain character values (.e. "name" unit). treatment Character; name single column containing variable treatment dummy indicator. column expected contain integer values, particular, equal 0 unit untreated time 1 otherwise. Treatment absorbing state; , unit treated time t, must also treated times t + 1, ..., T. units treated first time period removed automatically. Please make sure least units remain untreated final time period ("never-treated units"). response Character; name single column containing response unit time. response must integer numeric value. covs (Optional.) Either character vector containing names columns covariates (e.g., covs = c("x1", "x2")), one-sided formula (e.g., covs = ~ x1 + x2) – formula form mirrors convention used ::att_gt(xformla = ...). additive bare variable names supported formula form; derived variables, compute data frame first pass via character-vector form. columns expected contain integer, numeric, factor values, categorical values automatically encoded binary indicators. covariates provided, treatment effect estimation proceed, valid unconditional versions parallel trends anticipation assumptions. Default c(). indep_counts (Optional.) Integer; vector. sufficiently large number units, can optionally randomly split data set half (N units data set). data half units go pdata argument provided . N units, simply provide counts many units appear untreated cohort plus G cohorts argument indep_counts. benefit standard error average treatment effect (asymptotically) exact instead conservative. length indep_counts must equal 1 plus number treated cohorts pdata. entries indep_counts must strictly positive (concerned might work , maybe data set small side best just leave full data set pdata). sum counts indep_counts must match total number units pdata. Default NA (case conservative standard errors calculated q < 1.) sig_eps_sq (Optional.) Numeric; variance row-level IID noise assumed apply observation. See Section 2 Faletto (2025) details. best provide variance known (example, using simulated data). variance unknown, argument can omitted, variance estimated REML linear mixed-effects model y ~ X + (1 | unit) via lme4::lmer (Bates et al. 2015; Patterson & Thompson 1971). Default NA. sig_eps_c_sq (Optional.) Numeric; variance unit-level IID noise (random effects) assumed apply observation. See Section 2 Faletto (2025) details. best provide variance known (example, using simulated data). variance unknown, argument can omitted, variance estimated REML via lme4::lmer linear mixed-effects model y ~ X + (1 | unit) (Bates et al. 2015; Patterson & Thompson 1971). Default NA. lambda.max (Optional.) Numeric. penalty parameter lambda selected grid search BIC order select single model. largest lambda grid lambda.max. lambda.max provided, one selected automatically. q <= 1, model sparse, ideally following true : smallest model (one corresponding lambda.max) selects close 0 features, largest model (one corresponding lambda.min) selects close p features, nlambda large enough models considered every feasible model size, nlambda small enough computation become infeasible. may want manually tweak lambda.max, lambda.min, nlambda try achieve goals, particularly selected model size close model corresponding lambda.max lambda.min, indicate range lambda values narrow coarse. can use function outputs lambda.max_model_size, lambda.min_model_size, lambda_star_model_size try assess . Default NA. lambda.min (Optional.) Numeric. smallest lambda penalty parameter considered. See description lambda.max details. Default NA. nlambda (Optional.) Integer. total number lambda penalty parameters considered. See description lambda.max details. Default 100. q (Optional.) Numeric; determines L_q penalty used regularization. q = 1 lasso, 0 < q < 1, possible get standard errors confidence intervals. q = 2 ridge regression. See Faletto (2025) details. Default 0.5. verbose Logical; TRUE, details progress function printed function executes. Default FALSE. alpha Numeric; function calculate (1 - alpha) confidence intervals cohort average treatment effects returned catt_df. add_ridge (Optional.) Logical; TRUE, adds small amount ridge regularization (untransformed) coefficients stabilize estimation. Default FALSE. allow_no_never_treated (Optional.) Logical; TRUE (default) input panel contains never-treated units, panel auto-truncated dropping time periods latest cohort's start time — units latest cohort serve never-treated comparison group retained sub-panel — warning naming dropped periods. FALSE, estimator stops error case (package's behavior prior version 1.5.6). argument effect input already contains never-treated units. Default TRUE. se_type Character; one "default", "conservative", "cluster". "default" returns tight Gaussian variance sqrt(att_var_1 + att_var_2) Theorem (c$'$) Assumption (Psi-); asymptotically exact package's default cohort sample-proportions estimator every standard propensity-score estimator satisfies (Psi-) (multinomial logit, GLM W | X, kernel/series regression 1{W = g} X). "conservative" returns Cauchy-Schwarz upper bound Theorem (c); use propensity-score estimator violates (Psi-). "cluster" experimental unit-clustered Liang-Zeger sandwich SE bridge-selected support (see companion vignette inference_vignette details). "cluster" meaningful q < 1 (bridge oracle property required); q >= 1 SE NA regardless se_type. default "default" conservative Cauchy-Schwarz formula versions <= 1.11.7; v1.12.0 switched default tight Gaussian variance. Default "default". lambda_selection Character; method selecting bridge penalty parameter lambda. Either "cv" (10-fold cross-validation cv.grpreg; v1.13.0+ default) "bic" (BIC grpreg lambda grid; prior default v1.12.0 earlier). default changed v1.13.0 address finite-sample bias issue documented simulation studies (see issue #164). Pass lambda_selection = "bic" recover prior behavior. See inference vignette section "Choosing bridge penalty parameter" details. cv_folds Integer; number folds CV path. Ignored lambda_selection = "bic". Default 10. cv_seed Integer NULL; seed passed set.seed() immediately cv.grpreg() call. NULL (default), seed defaults internally .integer(N * T). Ignored lambda_selection = "bic". ci_type Character; one "simultaneous" (default) "pointwise". Controls confidence-interval bounds reported cohort-specific ATTs (catt_df) event-study effects (eventStudy(), shown print / summary / plot, surfaced broom::tidy() fitted object eventStudy() / cohortStudy() outputs). "simultaneous" reports parametric simultaneous (family-wise, uniform) bands computed via simultaneousCIs(): family's band covers effects jointly probability 1 - alpha, matching default presentation ::aggte(cband = TRUE). "pointwise" reports per-effect Wald intervals (covers effect probability 1 - alpha, joint guarantee — behavior versions <= 1.15.1). interval bounds per-cohort p-values (p_value) follow ci_type: "simultaneous" p_value single-step max-T multiplicity- adjusted p-value matching band, "pointwise" per-cohort Wald p-value (#200). standard errors (se) selection flags (selected) identical settings, overall-ATT confidence interval (single scalar) unaffected. standard errors unavailable (q >= 1, rank-deficient design) bounds NA settings. Default "simultaneous".

Bates, D., Maechler, M., Bolker, B., & Walker, S. (2015). Fitting Linear Mixed-Effects Models Using lme4. Journal Statistical Software, 67(1), 1-48. doi:10.18637/jss.v067.i01 . Patterson, H. title: Bridge-penalized extended two-way fixed effects — betwfe
text: Gregory Faletto

title: Bridge-penalized extended two-way fixed effects — betwfe
code: # `bacondecomp` (which supplies the `castle` data) is a Suggests-only # dependency, so guard the example on its availability. if (requireNamespace("bacondecomp", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(bacondecomp)    data(castle)    # Response: the log homicide rate. Treatment: `cdl` records the share of   # the year the castle-doctrine law was in effect, so `cdl > 0` gives the   # absorbing 0/1 treatment indicator. No `covs`: castle's smallest   # adoption cohorts contain a single state, so the design is   # rank-deficient once any covariate is added.   castle$l_homicide <- log(castle$homicide)   castle$treated <- as.integer(castle$cdl > 0)    # On this panel betwfe's bridge penalty selects every cohort out, so the   # estimated ATT and cohort effects below are all zero.   res <- betwfe(       pdata = castle,       time_var = "year",       unit_var = "state",       treatment = "treated",       response = "l_homicide",       verbose = TRUE)    # Average treatment effect on the treated units (in percentage point   # units)   100 * res$att_hat    # Conservative 95% confidence interval for ATT (in percentage point units)    low_att <- 100 * (res$att_hat - qnorm(1 - 0.05 / 2) * res$att_se)   high_att <- 100 * (res$att_hat + qnorm(1 - 0.05 / 2) * res$att_se)    c(low_att, high_att)    # Cohort average treatment effects and confidence intervals (in percentage   # point units)    catt_df_pct <- res$catt_df   catt_df_pct[["estimate"]] <- 100 * catt_df_pct[["estimate"]]   catt_df_pct[["se"]] <- 100 * catt_df_pct[["se"]]   catt_df_pct[["ci_low"]] <- 100 * catt_df_pct[["ci_low"]]   catt_df_pct[["ci_high"]] <- 100 * catt_df_pct[["ci_high"]]    catt_df_pct } #> No covariates provided; skipping covariate processing. #> Getting omega sqrt inverse estimate... #> Done! Time to estimate noise variances: #> 0.241447687149048 #> Time to get sqrt inverse matrix: #> 0.000263214111328125 #> Estimating bridge regression with 10-fold CV... #> Done! Time for estimation: #> 0.110379457473755 #> No treatment features selected; all treatment effects estimated to be 0. #>   cohort estimate se ci_low ci_high p_value selected #> 1   2005        0  0      0       0      NA    FALSE #> 2   2006        0  0      0       0      NA    FALSE #> 3   2007        0  0      0       0      NA    FALSE #> 4   2008        0  0      0       0      NA    FALSE #> 5   2009        0  0      0       0      NA    FALSE

"pointwise" reports per-effect Wald intervals (covers effect probability 1 - alpha, joint guarantee — behavior versions <= 1.15.1). interval bounds per-cohort p-values (p_value) follow ci_type: "simultaneous" p_value single-step max-T multiplicity- adjusted p-value matching band, "pointwise" per-cohort Wald p-value (#200). standard errors (se) selection flags (selected) identical settings, overall-ATT confidence interval (single scalar) unaffected. standard errors unavailable (q >= 1, rank-deficient design) bounds NA settings. Default "simultaneous".

title: Per-(cohort, time) average treatment effects — cohortTimeATTs
text: Extracts fully disaggregated treatment-effect estimates fitted FETWFE / ETWFE / BETWFE object: one row every (cohort, time) cell, averaging cohorts event time. finest-grained view estimated effects — num_treats underlying parameters — complementing cohortStudy() (averages cohort's cells time) eventStudy() (averages cohorts event time). Like eventStudy(), accessor available twfeCovs() objects: estimator single treatment-effect parameter per cohort (per-time disaggregation), finest granularity already cohortStudy(). Standard errors per-cell regression standard errors \(\sqrt{\sigma_\varepsilon^2 \, \psi' G^{-1} \psi / (NT)}\), recomputed call time fit's stored design (Gram-matrix machinery eventStudy() uses; nothing added fitted object). cell single cohort-time parameter, cohort-probability sampling variance contributes aggregated cohortStudy() / eventStudy() standard errors identically zero , cell's SE single coefficient's regression SE. Confidence intervals p-values pointwise \(1 - \alpha\) Wald quantities (estimate +/- z * se). simultaneous (family-wise) bands cell family, use simultaneousCIs(result, family = "all_post_treatment").

what: Debiased overall-ATT with a uniformly-valid standard error — debiasedATT
title: Debiased overall-ATT with a uniformly-valid standard error — debiasedATT
text: Computes debiased estimate overall average treatment effect treated (ATT) fitted fetwfe() object, together uniformly-valid standard error. complement fused plug-interval reported fit (fit$att_hat / fit$att_se): fused SE within-selection variance tends -cover aggregated overall ATT (omits -selection term), whereas debiasedATT() returns interval asymptotically nominal coverage roughly efficiency unrestricted ETWFE. debiased point estimate differs fused fit$att_hat: p < NT, OLS identity (paper eq. debiased.ols.identity) equals unrestricted ETWFE/OLS estimate ATT direction. therefore get dual offering — fit$att_hat / fit$att_se (fused, efficient, pointwise) debiasedATT(fit) (debiased, ETWFE-efficient, uniformly valid). exposed separate accessor (rather se_type option) precisely debiased SE accompanies different point estimate fused one. p >= NT unrestricted ETWFE infeasible, OLS identity longer applies; accessor instead builds debiasing direction nodewise (desparsified-lasso) relaxed inverse (Theorem debiased.highdim.thm Faletto (2025)). point estimate, SE formula, return value otherwise identical — "one estimator, two regimes." high-dimensional overall-ATT interval uniformly valid theorem, coverage validated near-nominally simulation (0.94 p >= NT anchor Faletto (2025), robust across nodewise penalty scales studied); see Assumptions.

title: Debiased overall-ATT with a uniformly-valid standard error — debiasedATT
text: uniformly-valid SE relies hypotheses paper Theorem debiased.att.thm: Marginal cohort assignment / (Psi-). Cohort membership independent outcome noise, regression cohort-weight variance channels uncorrelated add. package's default cohort weight estimator (sample proportions) satisfies . confounded assignment (cohort membership correlated treatment-effect heterogeneity) two channels correlate additive SE can mis-state uncertainty; combined per-unit score required (yet implemented). Correctly specified conditional mean outcome. Neyman-orthogonality removes first-order sensitivity selected nuisance coefficients, mean-misspecification. regression channel unit-clustered sandwich, valid number units N -> infinity model within-unit dependence (gls = FALSE fit, skips GLS whitening, yields valid cluster-robust SE; #307, paper Decision D1). GLS whitening (gls = TRUE) buys efficiency (asymptotically smaller var_reg), validity. (Low-dimensional p < NT ) Asymptotically negligible ridge, lambda = o((NT)^(-1/2)) (theoretical condition; leading case exact inverse lambda = 0). implementation use vanishing schedule — adds fixed numerical stabilizer 1e-6 * mean(diag(Sigma)) Gram solving, negligible cancels OLS-identity case. Growing number clusters, N -> infinity. CLT N independent units, NT rows. treated units cluster approximation poor interval can -cover; genuine -clusters correction (restricted wild-cluster bootstrap-t CR2-type analytic adjustment, #361) future work. method = "bootstrap" option remedy (see documentation). Regularity / two regimes. p < NT (Theorem debiased.att.thm) full design Gram nonsingular debiasing direction exact inverse; accessor reduces debiased ETWFE. p >= NT (high-dimensional FETWFE theory) Gram singular direction nodewise (desparsified-lasso) relaxed inverse equation debiased.highdim.v (estimate SE, v differs — "one estimator, two regimes"); requires sparsity (s_N log(p_N) / sqrt(N) -> 0), restricted-eigenvalue condition sparse cones, ||v*||_1 = O(1), bounded limiting variance ' Sigma_theta^(-1) . high-dimensional branch realizes Theorem debiased.highdim.thm (Faletto 2025) theory-scaled penalty; overall-ATT interval uniformly valid, coverage validated near-nominally simulation (0.94 p >= NT anchor, robust across nodewise penalty scales studied). Inspect returned feasibility / converged diagnostics confirm per-fit nodewise directions well-behaved. two regimes use different nuisances. fixed-p branch uses fit's bridge (q < 1) theta_hat: Theorem debiased.att.thm needs consistency nuisance, exact inverse OLS identity. high-dimensional branch instead fits internal q = 1 fused lasso (grpreg::cv.grpreg(penalty = "gBridge", gamma = 1), CV-selected lambda) nuisance, high-dimensional FETWFE theory controls orthogonalization remainder nuisance's l1 rate (||theta_hat - theta*||_1 = O_p(s_N lambda_theta)) — rate q = 1 fused lasso enjoys restricted-eigenvalue condition (standard desparsified-lasso rate; cf. Negahban et al. 2012), p >= NT extension paper points ; q < 1 bridge super-efficient / non-uniform — wrong nuisance uniformly-valid CI (#303). input fit must still q < 1 (supplies cohort-weight variance channel att_var_2); high-dimensional debiasing nuisance q = 1. nuisance's cross-validation uses fixed, data-derived seed, deterministic across calls (debiasedATT() high-dimensional simultaneousCIs() band center agree exactly) — tunable via fit's cv_seed.

title: Extended two-way fixed effects — etwfe
text: pdata Dataframe; panel data set. row represent observation unit time. contain columns described . time_var Character; name single column containing variable time period. column expected contain integer values (example, years). Recommended encodings dates include format YYYY, YYYYMM, YYYYMMDD, whichever appropriate data. unit_var Character; name single column containing variable unit. column expected contain character values (.e. "name" unit). treatment Character; name single column containing variable treatment dummy indicator. column expected contain integer values, particular, equal 0 unit untreated time 1 otherwise. Treatment absorbing state; , unit treated time t, must also treated times t + 1, ..., T. units treated first time period removed automatically. Please make sure least units remain untreated final time period ("never-treated units"). response Character; name single column containing response unit time. response must integer numeric value. covs (Optional.) Either character vector containing names columns covariates (e.g., covs = c("x1", "x2")), one-sided formula (e.g., covs = ~ x1 + x2) – formula form mirrors convention used ::att_gt(xformla = ...). additive bare variable names supported formula form; derived variables, compute data frame first pass via character-vector form. columns expected contain integer, numeric, factor values, categorical values automatically encoded binary indicators. covariates provided, treatment effect estimation proceed, valid unconditional versions parallel trends anticipation assumptions. Default c(). indep_counts (Optional.) Integer; vector. sufficiently large number units, can optionally randomly split data set half (N units data set). data half units go pdata argument provided . N units, simply provide counts many units appear untreated cohort plus G cohorts argument indep_counts. benefit standard error average treatment effect (asymptotically) exact instead conservative. length indep_counts must equal 1 plus number treated cohorts pdata. entries indep_counts must strictly positive (concerned might work , maybe data set small side best just leave full data set pdata). sum counts indep_counts must match total number units pdata. Default NA (case conservative standard errors calculated q < 1.) sig_eps_sq (Optional.) Numeric; variance row-level IID noise assumed apply observation. See Section 2 Faletto (2025) details. best provide variance known (example, using simulated data). variance unknown, argument can omitted, variance estimated REML linear mixed-effects model y ~ X + (1 | unit) via lme4::lmer (Bates et al. 2015; Patterson & Thompson 1971). Default NA. sig_eps_c_sq (Optional.) Numeric; variance unit-level IID noise (random effects) assumed apply observation. See Section 2 Faletto (2025) details. best provide variance known (example, using simulated data). variance unknown, argument can omitted, variance estimated REML via lme4::lmer linear mixed-effects model y ~ X + (1 | unit) (Bates et al. 2015; Patterson & Thompson 1971). Default NA. verbose Logical; TRUE, details progress function printed function executes. Default FALSE. alpha Numeric; function calculate (1 - alpha) confidence intervals cohort average treatment effects returned catt_df. add_ridge (Optional.) Logical; TRUE, adds small amount ridge regularization (untransformed) coefficients stabilize estimation. Default FALSE. allow_no_never_treated (Optional.) Logical; TRUE (default) input panel contains never-treated units, panel auto-truncated dropping time periods latest cohort's start time — units latest cohort serve never-treated comparison group retained sub-panel — warning naming dropped periods. FALSE, estimator stops error case (package's behavior prior version 1.5.6). argument effect input already contains never-treated units. Default TRUE. se_type Character; one "default", "conservative", "cluster". "default" returns tight Gaussian variance sqrt(att_var_1 + att_var_2) Theorem (c$'$) Assumption (Psi-); asymptotically exact package's default cohort sample-proportions estimator every standard propensity-score estimator satisfies (Psi-) (multinomial logit, GLM W | X, kernel/series regression 1{W = g} X). "conservative" returns Cauchy-Schwarz upper bound sqrt(att_var_1 + att_var_2 + 2 * sqrt(att_var_1 * att_var_2)) Theorem (c); use propensity-score estimator violates (Psi-) (e.g., Robins-Rotnitzky-augmented doubly-robust estimator, package currently implement). "cluster" experimental unit-clustered Liang-Zeger sandwich SE OLS-selected support (see companion vignette inference_vignette formula, assumptions, theory-pending caveat). default value "default" corresponds new tight Gaussian default introduced version 1.12.0; previous versions used conservative Cauchy-Schwarz formula default. recover prior conservative default behavior, pass se_type = "conservative". ci_type Character; one "simultaneous" (default) "pointwise". Controls confidence-interval bounds reported cohort-specific ATTs (catt_df) event-study effects (eventStudy(), shown print / summary / plot, surfaced broom::tidy() fitted object eventStudy() / cohortStudy() outputs). "simultaneous" reports parametric simultaneous (family-wise, uniform) bands computed via simultaneousCIs(): family's band covers effects jointly probability 1 - alpha, matching default presentation ::aggte(cband = TRUE). "pointwise" reports per-effect Wald intervals (covers effect probability 1 - alpha, joint guarantee — behavior versions <= 1.15.1). interval bounds per-cohort p-values (p_value) follow ci_type (max-T multiplicity-adjusted "simultaneous", per-cohort Wald "pointwise"; #200); standard errors (se) overall-ATT confidence interval (single scalar) identical settings. standard errors unavailable (e.g., rank-deficient design) bounds NA settings. Default "simultaneous".

title: Extended two-way fixed effects — etwfe
text: Gregory Faletto

title: Extended two-way fixed effects — etwfe
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(bacondecomp)  data(castle)  # Response: the log homicide rate. Treatment: `cdl` records the share of # the year the castle-doctrine law was in effect, so `cdl > 0` gives the # absorbing 0/1 treatment indicator. castle$l_homicide <- log(castle$homicide) castle$treated <- as.integer(castle$cdl > 0)  # No `covs` here: etwfe is pure OLS (no bridge penalty), and castle's # smallest adoption cohorts contain a single state, so the design is # rank-deficient once any covariate is added. res <- etwfe(     pdata = castle,     time_var = "year",     unit_var = "state",     treatment = "treated",     response = "l_homicide",     verbose = TRUE)  # Print results print(res, max_cohorts = Inf) } # }

what: Convert data prepared for etwfe::etwfe() to the format required by fetwfe() and fetwfe::etwfe() — etwfeToFetwfeDf

text: etwfeToFetwfeDf() reshapes renames panel dataset already formatted etwfe::etwfe() (McDermott 2024) can passed directly fetwfe() etwfe() fetwfe package. particular, creates absorbing-state treatment dummy equals 1 first treated period onward* 0 otherwise, (optionally) drops units already treated first period sample (fetwfe() removes internally), returns tidy dataframe whose column names match arguments fetwfe()/etwfe() expect.

Column name unit identifier (variable cluster , pass etwfe(..., ivar = idvar) using unit FEs). gvar Character. Column name "first treated" cohort variable passed etwfe() gvar. Must 0 never-treated units, (strictly positive) first treated period. covars Character vector additional covariate columns keep (default character(0)). drop_first_period_treated Logical. units already treated first sample period removed?  (fetwfe() drop internally anyway, keeps returned dataframe clean.)  Default TRUE. out_names Named list giving column names returned dataframe . default (time_var, unit_var, treatment, response) matches arguments usually supplied fetwfe(). change names list – values – keep four. verbose Logical. TRUE, message() reports count first-period-treated unit-period rows dropped drop_first_period_treated = TRUE. Default FALSE (silent).

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Generate coefficients   coefs <- genCoefs(G = 5, T = 30, d = 12, density = 0.1, eff_size = 2, seed = 123)    # Simulate data using the coefficients   sim_data <- simulateData(coefs, N = 120, sig_eps_sq = 5, sig_eps_c_sq = 5, seed = 123)    result <- etwfeWithSimulatedData(sim_data) } # }

what: Compute pooled event-time treatment-effect estimates — eventStudy
title: Compute pooled event-time treatment-effect estimates — eventStudy
text: fitted object fetwfe(), etwfe(), betwfe(), computes pooled event-time treatment-effect estimates tau_E(e), defined cohort-weighted averages cell-level treatment-effect estimates post-treatment event time e = t - g (t calendar time g cohort's first-treated calendar time). Weights sample-cohort-size weights (matching ::aggte(type = "dynamic") convention). Standard errors combine two terms, mirroring package's existing overall-ATT SE machinery: var_1(e) regression-coefficient noise (computed via gram_inv machinery package uses cohort SEs, cluster-robust sandwich se_type = "cluster"), var_2(e) cohort-probability noise (analog existing getSecondVarTermOLS / getSecondVarTermDataApp machinery, multinomial Jacobian restricted cohorts valid event time e). Combined sqrt(var_1 + var_2) default (asymptotically exact paper Theorem te.asym.norm.thm(\(c'\)) / Assumption (\(\Psi\)-), package's default cohort-sample-proportions estimator satisfies); conservative Cauchy-Schwarz bound sqrt(var_1 + var_2 + 2 sqrt(var_1 * var_2)) available via se_type = "conservative" (users non-(\(\Psi\)-) propensity-score estimators). indep_counts supplied fit time, tight formula applies regardless se_type (two-sample regime, Theorem (b)).

title: Compute pooled event-time treatment-effect estimates — eventStudy
code: eventStudy(x, alpha = NULL, ci_type = NULL)

title: Compute pooled event-time treatment-effect estimates — eventStudy
text: x fitted object class "fetwfe", "etwfe", "betwfe". alpha (Optional) Significance level confidence intervals. Defaults x$alpha (alpha used fit time). ci_type (Optional) Character; one "simultaneous" "pointwise", NULL (default). Controls whether returned ci_low / ci_high event-study-family simultaneous (family-wise, uniform) band per-event-time pointwise Wald intervals. NULL inherits fit's stored ci_type (fit made default ci_type = "simultaneous" yields simultaneous event-study bands, print / summary / plot display); objects fitted version 1.16.0 (ci_type slot) NULL falls back "pointwise". se column identical settings; interval bounds, critical-value multiplier, p_value differ ("simultaneous" p_value single-step max-T multiplicity- adjusted dual band, "pointwise" per-effect Wald p-value; #200). Degenerate event times (valid contributing cohorts) carry NA bounds NA p_value settings. High-dimensional (p >= NT): "simultaneous" band analytic post-selection band selected support (NA bounds gls = FALSE fit), uniformly-valid desparsified band. valid high-dimensional event-study band call simultaneousCIs() family = "event_study", method = "bootstrap" (Theorem debiased.highdim.joint.thm, validated near-nominally Faletto 2025).

title: Compute pooled event-time treatment-effect estimates — eventStudy
text: data frame class c("eventStudy", "data.frame") columns: event_time Integer; event time e = t - g, ranging 0 T - 2. n_cohorts Integer; number cohorts contributing pooled estimate event time e. estimate Numeric; pooled event-time ATT estimate. se Numeric; combined standard error. ci_low Numeric; lower bound (1 - alpha) Wald CI. ci_high Numeric; upper bound (1 - alpha) Wald CI. p_value Numeric; follows fit's ci_type. "pointwise", two-sided Wald p-value (2 * pnorm(-|estimate / se|)); "simultaneous" (default), single-step max-T multiplicity-adjusted (family-wise) p-value matching simultaneous band (#200). NA se 0 NA. post-treatment event times (e >= 0) included; pre-treatment placebo periods require extended regression specification scope initial release.

title: Compute pooled event-time treatment-effect estimates — eventStudy
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   coefs <- genCoefs(G = 3, T = 6, d = 2, density = 0.5, eff_size = 2)   dat <- simulateData(coefs, N = 120, sig_eps_sq = 1, sig_eps_c_sq = 0.5, seed = 123)   res <- fetwfeWithSimulatedData(dat)   eventStudy(res) } # }

what: Fused extended two-way fixed effects — fetwfe

text: Implementation fused extended two-way fixed effects. Estimates overall ATT well CATT (cohort average treatment effects treated units). treatment-effect fusion penalty defaults within-/-cohort geometry (fusion_structure = "cohort") also supports event-study geometry (fusion_structure = "event_study", fusing effects time since treatment across cohorts) fully custom fusion_matrix. See fusion_structure / fusion_matrix arguments vignette("fusion_structure_vignette", package = "fetwfe") guidance choosing.

"name" unit). treatment Character; name single column containing variable treatment dummy indicator. column expected contain integer values, particular, equal 0 unit untreated time 1 otherwise. Default c(). indep_counts (Optional.) Integer; vector. sufficiently large number units, can optionally randomly split data set half (N units data set). data half units go pdata argument provided . N units, simply provide counts many units appear untreated cohort plus G cohorts argument indep_counts. benefit standard error average treatment effect (asymptotically) exact instead conservative. length indep_counts must equal 1 plus number treated cohorts pdata. entries indep_counts must strictly positive (concerned might work , maybe data set small side best just leave full data set pdata). sum counts indep_counts must match total number units pdata. See Section 2 Faletto (2025) details. best provide variance known (example, using simulated data). variance unknown, argument can omitted, variance estimated REML linear mixed-effects model y ~ X + (1 | unit) via lme4::lmer (Bates et al. 2015; Patterson & Thompson 1971).
