Integer. total number lambda penalty parameters considered. See description lambda.max details. Default 100. q (Optional.) Numeric; determines L_q penalty used fusion regularization. q = 1 lasso, 0 < q < 1, possible get standard errors confidence intervals. q = 2 ridge regression. See Faletto (2025) details. Default 0.5. verbose Logical; TRUE, details progress function printed function executes. Default FALSE. alpha Numeric; function calculate (1 - alpha) confidence intervals cohort average treatment effects returned catt_df. add_ridge (Optional.) Logical; TRUE, adds small amount ridge regularization (untransformed) coefficients stabilize estimation. Default FALSE. allow_no_never_treated (Optional.) Logical; TRUE (default) input panel contains never-treated units, panel auto-truncated dropping time periods latest cohort's start time — units latest cohort serve never-treated comparison group retained sub-panel — warning naming dropped periods. FALSE, estimator stops error case (package's behavior prior version 1.5.6). argument effect input already contains never-treated units. Default TRUE. se_type Character; one "default", "conservative", "cluster". "default" returns tight Gaussian variance sqrt(att_var_1 + att_var_2) Theorem (c$'$) Assumption (Psi-); asymptotically exact package's default cohort sample-proportions estimator every standard propensity-score estimator satisfies (Psi-) (multinomial logit, GLM W | X, kernel/series regression 1{W = g} X). "conservative" returns Cauchy-Schwarz upper bound sqrt(att_var_1 + att_var_2 + 2 * sqrt(att_var_1 * att_var_2)) Theorem (c); use propensity-score estimator violates (Psi-) (e.g., Robins-Rotnitzky-augmented doubly-robust estimator, package currently implement). "cluster" experimental unit-clustered Liang-Zeger sandwich SE bridge-selected support (see companion vignette inference_vignette formula, assumptions, theory-pending caveat); meaningful q < 1 (bridge oracle property required), q >= 1 SE NA regardless se_type. default value "default" corresponds new tight Gaussian default introduced version 1.12.0; previous versions used conservative Cauchy-Schwarz formula default. recover prior conservative default behavior, pass se_type = "conservative". lambda_selection Character; method selecting bridge penalty parameter lambda. Either "cv" (10-fold cross-validation cv.grpreg; v1.13.0+ default) "bic" (BIC grpreg lambda grid; prior default v1.12.0 earlier). default changed v1.13.0 address finite-sample bias issue documented simulation studies (see issue #164): prior BIC default, overall-ATT estimator biased toward zero moderate sample sizes, producing 95% confidence intervals whose empirical coverage low 0.00 regimes. Cross-validation restores near-nominal coverage every regime tested. recover prior behavior — example, reproducing analyses run v1.12.0 earlier — pass lambda_selection = "bic". See inference vignette section "Choosing bridge penalty parameter" details. cv_folds Integer; number folds CV path. Ignored lambda_selection = "bic". Default 10. cv_seed Integer NULL; seed passed set.seed() immediately cv.grpreg() call, controlling fold assignment. NULL (default), seed defaults internally .integer(N * T) consecutive calls dataset reproducible without user specify seed. seed actually used stored returned object cv_seed. Ignored lambda_selection = "bic". ci_type Character; one "simultaneous" (default) "pointwise". Controls confidence-interval bounds reported cohort-specific ATTs (catt_df) event-study effects (eventStudy(), shown print / summary / plot, surfaced broom::tidy() fitted object eventStudy() / cohortStudy() outputs). "simultaneous" reports parametric simultaneous (family-wise, uniform) bands computed via simultaneousCIs(): family's band covers effects jointly probability 1 - alpha, matching default presentation ::aggte(cband = TRUE). "pointwise" reports per-effect Wald intervals (covers effect probability 1 - alpha, joint guarantee — behavior versions <= 1.15.1). interval bounds per-cohort p-values (p_value) follow ci_type: "simultaneous" p_value single-step max-T multiplicity- adjusted p-value matching band, "pointwise" per-cohort Wald p-value (#200). standard errors (se) selection flags (selected) identical settings, overall-ATT confidence interval (single scalar) unaffected. standard errors unavailable (q >= 1, rank-deficient design) bounds NA settings. Default "simultaneous". fusion_structure Character; one "cohort" "event_study". "cohort" (default) uses within-cohort / -cohort two-way fusion penalty. "event_study" instead fuses treatment effects time since treatment (event time e = t - g) across cohorts. event-study penalty carries theoretical guarantees default (Faletto 2025); treatment-effect fusion structure changes. fusion_matrix (Optional.) Numeric matrix NULL (default). advanced-use override: user-supplied num_treats x num_treats forward differences matrix D_N treatment-effect block, encoding arbitrary fusion structure beyond two built-ins. non-NULL overrides fusion_structure treatment-effect block (fixed-effect blocks unchanged); estimator uses solve(fusion_matrix) internally. rows/columns interpreted cohort-major (g, t) order used internally treatment effects (order getFirstInds() / getTreatInds() encode): row/column corresponds base treatment effect , cohort g occupying rows first_inds[g]:(first_inds[g + 1] - 1) ordered event time. num_treats equals T * G - G * (G + 1) / 2. fusion_matrix must finite, invertible numeric matrix exact dimension (otherwise fetwfe() stops). paper's fixed-dimension scoping, finite invertible D_N dimension inherits paper's inferential guarantees: theory depends D_N invertibility singular-value bounds (Assumption (D) Faletto 2025), fixed invertible matrix automatically satisfies, swapping different D_N class changes constant factors. numerically near-singular (ill-conditioned) D_N still yields valid point estimator emits warning() inverse may unreliable. Default NULL (use built-fusion_structure). gls (Optional.) Logical; default TRUE. TRUE, design GLS-whitened using REML-estimated (supplied) variance components — standard, efficient path. FALSE, fetwfe() skips GLS whitening variance-component estimation entirely, fitting un-whitened (fusion-transformed) design. high-dimensional (p >= NT) path: REML estimate variance components (p < N(T - 1) REML guard otherwise stop fit), whitening buys efficiency, validity — debiasedATT() cluster-robust sandwich standard error needs Omega (paper Decision D1). gls = FALSE fit calc_ses = FALSE (within-selection oracle standard errors) un-whitened internal$X_final / internal$y_final; pass debiasedATT() valid cluster-robust SE. add_ridge = TRUE supported, supplied sig_eps_sq / sig_eps_c_sq ignored, gls = FALSE.

title: Fused extended two-way fixed effects — fetwfe
text: object class fetwfe containing following elements: att_hat estimated overall average treatment effect randomly selected treated unit. att_se q < 1, standard error ATT. default se_type = "default", SE tight Gaussian variance sqrt(att_var_1 + att_var_2) (Theorem (c$'$) Assumption (Psi-); paper line 1233 onwards). Assumption (Psi-) satisfied package's default cohort sample-proportions estimator hat_pi_g = N_g / N (multinomial logit, GLM W | X, kernel/series regression 1{W = g} X), default SE asymptotically exact package's default estimator. se_type = "conservative" (version <= 1.11.7 default), SE Cauchy-Schwarz upper bound sqrt(att_var_1 + att_var_2 + 2 * sqrt(att_var_1 * att_var_2)) Theorem (c). indep_counts provided, two-sample exact formula sqrt(att_var_1 + att_var_2) used regardless se_type. q >= 1, NA. att_p_value two-sided p-value overall ATT null H_0: tau = 0, computed 2 * pnorm(-|att_hat / att_se|). NA att_se zero NA (e.g., bridge solver's selected-fallback). See package vignette section "Testing zero-effect null" interpretation guidance selection consistency. att_selected Logical scalar; TRUE att_hat exactly zero (.e., least one cohort's bridge-penalized coefficient survived selection), FALSE otherwise. FETWFE Theorem 6.2 (restriction selection consistency), att_selected = FALSE asymptotic statement truth zero. ridge (q = 2) bridge solver zero coefficients, typically TRUE. catt_hats named vector containing estimated average treatment effects cohort. catt_ses q < 1, named vector containing (asymptotically exact, non-conservative) standard errors estimated average treatment effects within cohort. cohort_probs vector estimated probabilities cohort conditional treated, used calculating att_hat. indep_counts provided, cohort_probs calculated ; otherwise, calculated counts units treated cohort pdata. catt_df data frame (S3 class c("catt_df", "data.frame")) displaying cohort names (cohort), average treatment effects (estimate), standard errors (se), 1 - alpha confidence interval bounds (ci_low, ci_high), per-cohort p-values (p_value), selected logical flag (TRUE bridge penalty left cohort's CATT nonzero). selected-cohorts (selected = FALSE), p_value NA — inferential content lives selected. catt_df S3 class makes [[ / $ / [ access pre-1.11.0 Title-Case column names (Cohort, Estimated TE, SE, ConfIntLow, ConfIntHigh, P_value) stop() migration message pointing new name. See NEWS.md rename table. beta_hat full vector estimated coefficients. treat_inds indices beta_hat corresponding treatment effects cohort time. treat_int_inds indices beta_hat corresponding interactions treatment effects cohort time covariates. sig_eps_sq Either provided sig_eps_sq estimated one, value provided. sig_eps_c_sq Either provided sig_eps_c_sq estimated one, value provided. lambda.max Either provided lambda.max one used, value provided. (returned help getting reasonable range lambda values grid search.) lambda.max_model_size number selected features (excluding always-present intercept) lambda.max (q <= 1, smallest model size). mentioned , q <= 1 ideally value close 0. lambda.min Either provided lambda.min one used, value provided. lambda.min_model_size number selected features (excluding always-present intercept) lambda.min (q <= 1, largest model size). mentioned , q <= 1 ideally value close p. lambda_star value lambda chosen selection method recorded lambda_selection. value close lambda.min lambda.max, suggest range lambda values expanded. lambda_star_model_size number selected features (excluding always-present intercept) chosen model. value close lambda.max_model_size lambda.min_model_size, suggest range lambda values expanded. lambda_selection Character scalar; either "cv" (10-fold cross-validation cv.grpreg; v1.13.0+ default) "bic" (BIC grpreg lambda grid; prior default). Mirrors lambda_selection argument user passed. cv_folds Integer scalar; cv_folds value used lambda_selection = "cv", NA_integer_ lambda_selection = "bic". cv_seed Integer scalar; seed actually fed set.seed() immediately cv.grpreg() called. Defaults .integer(N * T) user pass seed. NA_integer_ lambda_selection = "bic". fusion_structure Character scalar; fusion_structure argument user passed ("cohort" "event_study"), recording fusion-penalty differences matrix used treatment effects. fusion_matrix user-supplied custom forward differences matrix D_N (num_treats x num_treats numeric matrix), NULL none supplied. non-NULL overrode fusion_structure treatment-effect block; estimator used solve(fusion_matrix) internally. See fusion_matrix argument. N final number units data set used estimation (units may removed treated first time period). T number time periods final data set. G final number treated cohorts appear final data set. R Deprecated alias G, retained backward compatibility; populated value. Use G. removed future release. d final number covariates appear final data set (covariates may removed contained missing values contained value every unit). p final number columns full set covariates used estimate model. alpha alpha level used confidence intervals. calc_ses Logical indicating whether standard errors calculated. $internal$calc_ses; duplicated top level parity etwfe(), betwfe(), twfeCovs() (#180). cohort_probs_overall vector estimated cohort probabilities overall sample (treated untreated), used computing variance overall ATT. indep_counts_used Logical scalar; TRUE valid indep_counts argument provided used asymptotically-exact ATT inference, FALSE otherwise. se_type Character scalar; se_type argument user passed ("default", "conservative", "cluster"). ci_type Character scalar; ci_type argument user passed ("simultaneous" "pointwise"), controlling whether reported catt_df / eventStudy() confidence-interval bounds simultaneous (family-wise) pointwise. y_mean Numeric scalar; mean original (pre-centering) response. Stored downstream methods (augment(), predict()) can return fitted values original-response scale. response_col_name Character scalar; name response column original pdata. Consumed augment.<class>(). time_var, unit_var, treatment Character scalars; time_var / unit_var / treatment arguments user passed. Consumed augment.<class>() auto-aligning user-supplied panel fitted design (e.g., dropping first-period-treated units estimator removed internally, sorting rows match design matrix's internal (unit, time) order). covs Character vector; original covs argument user passed (factor expansion estimator performed internally). Consumed augment.<class>(). internal list containing internal outputs typically needed interpretation: X_ints design matrix created containing interactions, time cohort dummies, etc. y vector responses, containing nrow(X_ints) entries. X_final design matrix applying change coordinates fit model also multiplying left square root inverse estimated covariance matrix unit. y_final final response multiplying left square root inverse estimated covariance matrix unit. theta_hat vector estimated coefficients transformed (fused) space, including intercept first element. calc_ses Logical indicating whether standard errors calculated. variance_components list exposing two variance pieces (att_var_1, att_var_2) plus paper-notation counterparts (V_1, V_2) unit-scaled variance estimators (tilde_v_N, hat_v_N, tilde_v_N_C, tilde_v_N_C_pi_hat, tilde_v_N_C_pi_hat_cons, tilde_v_N_cons) catalogued paper line 2006. Wald CI [hat_T_N +- qnorm(1-alpha/2) * sqrt(tilde_v_N / N)] (paper Eq. conf.int.form). New v1.12.0 (issue #141 + #146). first_year Integer numeric scalar; first (earliest) time_var value panel idCohorts() processing. Consumed eventStudy() map cohort_probs' cohort labels (treatment-start years) 1-based panel-time-index offsets labels integer-coercible. New v1.13.3 (issue #174). d_inv_treat inverted custom treatment-effect fusion block solve(fusion_matrix) (num_treats x num_treats numeric matrix), NULL fusion_matrix supplied. Consumed eventStudy() simultaneousCIs() access-time bands reuse fusion block fit used (#236). object methods print(), summary(), coef(). default, print() summary() show essential outputs. see internal details, use print(x, show_internal = TRUE) summary(x, show_internal = TRUE). coef() method returns vector estimated coefficients (beta_hat).

title: Fused extended two-way fixed effects — fetwfe
code: # `bacondecomp` (which supplies the `divorce` data) is a Suggests-only # dependency, so guard the example on its availability. The fit is wrapped in # \donttest{} because it is slower than a toy example. # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("bacondecomp", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(bacondecomp)    data(divorce)    # Stevenson & Wolfers (2006): the effect of unilateral ("no-fault") divorce   # reforms on female suicide rates. Restrict to the female subset   # (`sex == 2`); `changed` is already the absorbing 0/1 reform indicator, and   # the elasticity-scaled female suicide rate is the response.   divorce_f <- divorce[divorce$sex == 2, ]    # Reproduces the empirical application in Faletto (2025, Sec. 8.2). The 9   # states already treated by 1964 are auto-dropped as first-period-treated,   # and `murderrate` is auto-dropped (missing in 1964 for one state); both are   # reported as (expected) warnings. The noise variances are supplied   # (precomputed by REML) to keep the example fast and reproducible; the   # default lambda_selection is "cv" (10-fold cross-validation).   res <- fetwfe(       pdata = divorce_f,       time_var = "year",       unit_var = "st",       treatment = "changed",       covs = c("murderrate", "lnpersinc", "afdcrolls"),       response = "suiciderate_elast_jag",       sig_eps_sq = 0.0344,       sig_eps_c_sq = 0.1507,       add_ridge = TRUE,       q = 0.5)    # FETWFE estimates an overall ATT of roughly -6% on the elasticity-scaled   # female suicide rate, with a 95% confidence interval that excludes zero.   # The selection step retains heterogeneous cohort effects (several cohorts   # are pruned to exactly zero), rather than fusing to a single common effect.   print(res, max_cohorts = Inf) } #> Warning: 9 units were removed because they were treated in the first time period: AK, LA, MD, NC, OK, UT, VA, VT, WV #> Warning: 1 covariate(s) were removed because they contained missing values in the first time period for at least one unit:  murderrate #> Fused Extended Two-Way Fixed Effects Results #> =========================================== #>  #> Overall Average Treatment Effect (ATT): #>   Estimate:   -0.0602 #>   Std. Error: 0.0188 #>   P-value:    0.001376 #>   Selected:   TRUE #>   95% CI:    [-0.0970, -0.0233] #>  #> Cohort Average Treatment Effects (CATT) [simultaneous 95% CI]: #>  cohort    estimate Default NULL (use built-fusion_structure). gls (Optional.) Logical; default TRUE. TRUE, design GLS-whitened using REML-estimated (supplied) variance components — standard, efficient path. FALSE, fetwfe() skips GLS whitening variance-component estimation entirely, fitting un-whitened (fusion-transformed) design. high-dimensional (p >= NT) path: REML estimate variance components (p < N(T - 1) REML guard otherwise stop fit), whitening buys efficiency, validity — debiasedATT() cluster-robust sandwich standard error needs Omega (paper Decision D1). gls = FALSE fit calc_ses = FALSE (within-selection oracle standard errors) un-whitened internal$X_final / internal$y_final; pass debiasedATT() valid cluster-robust SE. add_ridge = TRUE supported, supplied sig_eps_sq / sig_eps_c_sq ignored, gls = FALSE.

title: Run FETWFE on Simulated Data — fetwfeWithSimulatedData

Assumption (Psi-) satisfied package's default cohort sample-proportions estimator hat_pi_g = N_g / N (multinomial logit, GLM W | X, kernel/series regression 1{W = g} X), default SE asymptotically exact package's default estimator. se_type = "conservative" (version <= 1.11.7 default), SE Cauchy-Schwarz upper bound sqrt(att_var_1 + att_var_2 + 2 * sqrt(att_var_1 * att_var_2)) Theorem (c). indep_counts provided, two-sample exact formula sqrt(att_var_1 + att_var_2) used regardless se_type. q >= 1, NA. att_p_value two-sided p-value overall ATT null H_0: tau = 0, computed 2 * pnorm(-|att_hat / att_se|). NA att_se zero NA (e.g., bridge solver's selected-fallback). See package vignette section "Testing zero-effect null" interpretation guidance selection consistency. att_selected Logical scalar; TRUE att_hat exactly zero (.e., least one cohort's bridge-penalized coefficient survived selection), FALSE otherwise. FETWFE Theorem 6.2 (restriction selection consistency), att_selected = FALSE asymptotic statement truth zero. ridge (q = 2) bridge solver zero coefficients, typically TRUE. catt_hats named vector containing estimated average treatment effects cohort. catt_ses q < 1, named vector containing (asymptotically exact, non-conservative) standard errors estimated average treatment effects within cohort. cohort_probs vector estimated probabilities cohort conditional treated, used calculating att_hat. indep_counts provided, cohort_probs calculated ; otherwise, calculated counts units treated cohort pdata. catt_df data frame (S3 class c("catt_df", "data.frame")) displaying cohort names (cohort), average treatment effects (estimate), standard errors (se), 1 - alpha confidence interval bounds (ci_low, ci_high), per-cohort p-values (p_value), selected logical flag (TRUE bridge penalty left cohort's CATT nonzero). selected-cohorts (selected = FALSE), p_value NA — inferential content lives selected. catt_df S3 class makes [[ / $ / [ access pre-1.11.0 Title-Case column names (Cohort, Estimated TE, SE, ConfIntLow, ConfIntHigh, P_value) stop() migration message pointing new name. what: Generate Coefficient Vector for Data Generation — genCoefs
title: Generate Coefficient Vector for Data Generation — genCoefs
text: function generates coefficient vector beta simulation studies fused extended two-way fixed effects estimator. returns S3 object class "FETWFE_coefs" containing beta along simulation parameters G, T, d. See simulation studies section Faletto (2025) details.

title: Generate Coefficient Vector for Data Generation — genCoefs
code: genCoefs(   G = NULL,   T,   d,   density,   eff_size,   fusion_structure = c("cohort", "event_study"),   assignment_type = c("marginal", "multinomial", "ordered"),   assignment_strength = 1,   assignment_interactions = NULL,   assignment_interaction_strength = NULL,   n_signal_cohorts = NULL,   treat_base_levels = NULL,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   R = NULL )

title: Generate Coefficient Vector for Data Generation — genCoefs
text: G Optional integer. number treated cohorts (treatment assumed start periods 2 G + 1). Defaults NULL; supply either G deprecated alias R (described ). T Integer. total number time periods. d Integer. number time-invariant covariates. d > 0, additional terms corresponding covariate main effects interactions included beta. density Numeric (0,1]. probability given entry initial coefficient vector theta nonzero. density = 1 gives fully dense (non-sparse) coefficient vector. eff_size Numeric. magnitude used scale nonzero entries theta. nonzero entry set eff_size -eff_size (60 percent chance positive value). fusion_structure Character. One "cohort" (default) "event_study". Controls basis true treatment-effect coefficients sparse. "cohort" sparse transformed coefficients theta mapped back default two-way (cohort) inverse fusion transform (byte-identical previous behavior); "event_study" mapped event-study inverse fusion transform, true treatment effects sparse event-study basis (effects sharing time since treatment, \(e = t - g\), tend equal across cohorts). simulation-side companion fusion_structure argument fetwfe(). assignment_type Character. One "marginal" (default), "multinomial", "ordered". Selects data-generating process cohort assignment simulateData(). "marginal": unit's cohort drawn uniformly, independent covariates. Original pre-1.14.0 behavior, preserved byte-identically. "multinomial": cohort assignment follows multinomial-logit propensity-score model \(\pi_g(x) = \exp(\gamma_g^\top x) / \sum_{g'} \exp(\gamma_{g'}^\top x)\) \(\gamma_0 \equiv 0\) (never-treated reference). Requires \(d \ge 1\). "ordered": cohort assignment follows proportional-odds (ordered-logit) model cumulative probabilities \(P(W \le g | x) = \mathrm{plogis}(\alpha_g - \gamma^\top x)\). Cutpoints \(\alpha_g\) chosen marginal cohort probabilities approximately uniform 1/(G + 1). Requires \(d \ge 1\). assignment_strength Non-negative numeric scalar. Scales logit coefficients propensity-score model. 0 reduces non-marginal types uniform marginal distribution construction. Larger values produce stronger covariate-cohort coupling. Defaults 1.0. Ignored assignment_type = "marginal". assignment_interactions Optional. list length-2 integer vectors, naming pair covariate indices \((j, k)\) \([1, d]\) whose elementwise product \(x_{,j} \cdot x_{,k}\) enters propensity model additional column. Self-interactions c(j, j) allowed yield quadratic term \(x_j^2\). Unordered pairs canonicalized c(min(j, k), max(j, k)) duplicates silently deduplicated (inspect coefs$assignment_coefs$interactions returned object verify retained list). interaction columns enter propensity model ; outcome model continues use original covariates. Defaults NULL (interactions — v1.14.0 behavior preserved byte-identically). Passing list() (empty list) treated equivalent NULL — interactions specified, behavior identical v1.14.0 marginal-cohort path within multinomial / ordered DGPs. Errors assignment_type = "marginal" (marginal DGP propensity model augment). New 1.14.1. assignment_interaction_strength Optional non-negative numeric scalar. Scales Gaussian draws interaction coefficients independently assignment_strength. Defaults NULL, means "fall assignment_strength". Useful stress-testing whether nonlinear-propensity angle alone drives downstream differences (without simultaneously cranking linear angle). Ignored assignment_interactions = NULL. New 1.14.1. n_signal_cohorts (Optional) Targeted-sparsity mode (#332). single positive integer k 1..(G - 1). supplied, coefficient vector built deterministically treatment signal placed exactly k cohorts' fused base levels (covariate / interaction blocks left zero), giving ||theta||_0 = k non-zeros guaranteed non-zero, heterogeneous cohort effect (overall ATT \(\neq 0\) positive cohort-weight variance \(V_2 > 0\)). produces simultaneously sparse non-degenerate high-dimensional (\(p \ge NT\)) data-generating process desparsified / nodewise debiased-ATT coverage study needs, default uniform density placement (almost never lands signal tiny treatment-effect coordinate block). Cohort 1 held baseline cohorts 2..(k + 1) receive distinct magnitudes eff_size * (1, 2, ..., k) (eff_size must non-zero). Defaults NULL; NULL (treat_base_levels = NULL) unchanged, byte-identical uniform-density behavior. Mutually exclusive treat_base_levels. treat_base_levels (Optional) Targeted-sparsity mode (#332), explicit form. finite numeric vector length G giving cohort's fused base level directly (placed getTreatInds()[getFirstInds()]); every coordinate left zero, ||theta||_0 number non-zero entries. Note fused base levels, per-cohort ATTs: fusion_structure = "cohort" vector (b_1, ..., b_G) maps cumulative cohort effects cumsum(b) (e.g. c(0, m, 0) gives cohort ATTs (0, m, m)), "event_study" uses different still heterogeneity-preserving map. result must non-degenerate heterogeneous (\(V_2 > 0\)); -zero constant-effect specification rejected. Defaults NULL. Mutually exclusive n_signal_cohorts. seed (Optional) Integer. Seed reproducibility. Three deterministic offsets share seed: main coefficient draw uses seed; assignment coefficients use seed + 1L; Monte Carlo integration getTes() uses seed + 2L. NA (NULL) means "draw ambient random-number generator" — set.seed() called, preceding set.seed() respected result varies across calls. verbose Logical. TRUE, emit message() assignment_interactions canonicalization removes duplicate unordered pairs (e.g., user passes c(1, 2) c(2, 1)). Default FALSE (silent — users can verify final canonical list via coefs$assignment_coefs$interactions). R Deprecated. former name G; still accepted deprecation warning, removed future release. Use G.

G provided number treated cohorts. R Deprecated alias G, retained backward compatibility; populated value. Use G. removed future release. T provided number time periods. d provided number covariates. seed provided seed. assignment_type selected cohort-assignment DGP ("marginal" / "multinomial" / "ordered"). New 1.14.0. assignment_strength scaling factor applied assignment coefficients. New 1.14.0. assignment_interaction_strength scaling factor applied interaction coefficients. New 1.14.1. assignment_coefs NULL assignment_type = "marginal"; otherwise list elements type, strength, coefs (gamma matrix vector), (ordered) cutpoints. Starting 1.14.1, assignment_coefs also carries sub-slots interactions (canonicalized + deduplicated list pairs, NULL), delta (interaction coefficient matrix multinomial vector ordered, NULL), interaction_strength (effective scaling factor used delta draws, NULL interactions). New 1.14.0; interactions, delta, interaction_strength sub-slots new 1.14.1.

title: Generate Coefficient Vector for Data Generation — genCoefs
text: Optional arguments assignment_type assignment_strength control whether cohort membership simulated panel drawn marginally (independent covariates, original behavior) covariate-dependent propensity-score model — either multinomial-logit ordered-logit (proportional-odds) model. default assignment_type = "marginal" preserves pre-1.14.0 behavior byte-identically. See vignette("simulation_vignette", package = "fetwfe") worked examples. length beta given $$p = G + (T - 1) + d + dG + d(T - 1) + \mathit{num\_treats} + (\mathit{num\_treats} \times d)$$, number treatment parameters defined $$\mathit{num\_treats} = T \times G - \frac{G(G+1)}{2}$$. function operates two steps: first creates sparse vector theta length \(p\), nonzero entries occurring probability density. Nonzero entries set eff_size -eff_size (60\ full coefficient vector beta computed applying inverse fusion transform theta using internal routines: genBackwardsInvFusionTransformMat() fixed-effect blocks , treatment-effect block, genInvTwoWayFusionTransformMat() fusion_structure = "cohort" genInvEventStudyFusionTransformMat() fusion_structure = "event_study". multinomial-logit proportional-odds reference DGPs canonical parametric propensity-score models named Faletto (2025) line 1016; propensity-weighted population-truth aggregation matches Eq. att.estimator.weighted (line 837). Note random-number generator: passing explicit numeric seed calls set.seed(seed) internally leaves global RNG advanced call returns. deliberate — makes simulation reproducible (seed always yields coefficients) varying (drawing data afterwards consumes advanced stream). draw / preserve ambient random stream instead — without calling set.seed() — pass seed = NA (seed = NULL).

title: Generate Coefficient Vector for Data Generation — genCoefs
text: Faletto, G (2025). Fused Extended Two-Way Fixed Effects Difference--Differences Staggered Adoptions. arXiv preprint arXiv:2312.05985. https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.05985.

title: Generate Coefficient Vector for Data Generation — genCoefs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Generate coefficients   coefs <- genCoefs(G = 5, T = 30, d = 12, density = 0.1, eff_size = 2, seed = 123)    # Simulate data using the coefficients   sim_data <- simulateData(coefs, N = 120, sig_eps_sq = 5, sig_eps_c_sq = 5, seed = 123)    # Event-study-sparse truth: treatment effects that share the same time   # since treatment are fused across cohorts (the simulation-side companion   # to fetwfe()'s fusion_structure = "event_study").   coefs_es <- genCoefs(     G = 5, T = 30, d = 12, density = 0.1, eff_size = 2,     fusion_structure = "event_study", seed = 123   )    # Covariate-dependent cohort assignment: multinomial-logit DGP   coefs_mn <- genCoefs(     G = 5, T = 30, d = 12, density = 0.1, eff_size = 2,     assignment_type = "multinomial", assignment_strength = 1.0,     seed = 123   )   sim_mn <- simulateData(coefs_mn, N = 120, sig_eps_sq = 5, sig_eps_c_sq = 5, seed = 123)    # Covariate-dependent cohort assignment with nonlinear propensity   # (multinomial-logit + a single x1*x2 interaction term in the propensity   # model only; outcome model continues to use plain X):   coefs_int <- genCoefs(     G = 5, T = 30, d = 12, density = 0.1, eff_size = 2,     assignment_type = "multinomial",     assignment_interactions = list(c(1, 2)),     assignment_interaction_strength = 1.5,     seed = 123   ) } # }

title: Generate Coefficient Vector for Data Generation (core) — genCoefsCore
text: Faletto, G (2025). Fused Extended Two-Way Fixed Effects Difference--Differences Staggered Adoptions. arXiv preprint arXiv:2312.05985. https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.05985.

title: Generate Coefficient Vector for Data Generation (core) — genCoefsCore
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Set parameters for the coefficient generation   G <- 3         # Number of treated cohorts   T <- 6         # Total number of time periods   d <- 2         # Number of covariates   density <- 0.1 # Probability that an entry in the initial vector is nonzero   eff_size <- 1.5  # Scaling factor for nonzero coefficients   seed <- 789    # Seed for reproducibility    # Generate coefficients using genCoefsCore()   coefs_core <- genCoefsCore(G = G, T = T, d = d, density = density,   eff_size = eff_size, seed = seed)   beta <- coefs_core$beta   theta <- coefs_core$theta    # For diagnostic purposes, compute the expected length of beta.   # The length p is defined internally as:   #   p = G + (T - 1) + d + d*G + d*(T - 1) + num_treats + num_treats*d,   # where num_treats = T * G - (G*(G+1))/2.   num_treats <- T * G - (G * (G + 1)) / 2   p_expected <- G + (T - 1) + d + d * G + d * (T - 1) + num_treats + num_treats * d    cat("Length of beta:", length(beta), "\nExpected length:", p_expected, "\n") } # }

title: Compute True Treatment Effects — getTes
text: function extracts true treatment effects full coefficient vector generated genCoefs(). returns per-cohort CATTs overall ATT. default marginal cohort-assignment DGP, overall ATT equal-weighted mean cohort-specific effects. covariate-dependent DGPs introduced 1.14.0, overall ATT propensity-weighted mean using cohort weights \(E[\pi_g(X)] / \sum_{g' \text{ treated}} E[\pi_{g'}(X)]\), matching Faletto (2025) Eq. att.estimator.weighted (line 837) population level. expected propensities computed Monte Carlo integration covariate distribution.

title: Compute True Treatment Effects — getTes
code: getTes(coefs_obj)

title: Compute True Treatment Effects — getTes
text: coefs_obj object class "FETWFE_coefs" containing coefficient vector simulation parameters.

title: Compute True Treatment Effects — getTes
text: object class "FETWFE_tes", list following elements: att_true numeric value representing overall average treatment effect treated. marginal DGP equal-weighted mean cohort-specific effects; covariate-dependent DGPs propensity-weighted mean using cohort_weights. actual_cohort_tes numeric vector length G containing true cohort-specific treatment effects, calculated averaging coefficients corresponding treatment dummies cohort. Intrinsic \(\beta\); depend assignment DGP. actual_event_time_tes named numeric vector length T - 1 giving true treatment effect event time (periods since adoption) e = 0, 1, ..., T - 2, names "0", ..., "T-2". event time's effect mean true per-(g, t) cell effects cohorts still observed event time, weighted proportion cohort_weights — aggregation eventStudy() estimates fitted panel. NA event time cohort reaches (eventStudy() instead reports 0). New 1.56.7. cohort_times integer vector length G giving calendar time period treated cohort first adopts treatment. simulator's convention cohort g adopts calendar time g + 1 (cohort 0 never-treated). cohort_weights Numeric vector length G summing 1. marginal DGP uniform 1/G. assignment_type = "multinomial" "ordered" \(E[\pi_g(X)] / \sum_{g' \text{ treated}} E[\pi_{g'}(X)]\). New 1.14.0. assignment_type Character; cohort-assignment DGP carried coefs_obj (one "marginal", "multinomial", "ordered"). Determines whether cohort_weights uniform (marginal) propensity-weighted. New 1.18.1. assignment_strength Numeric; assignment-strength scaling carried coefs_obj (meaningful assignment_type != "marginal"). NULL FETWFE_coefs objects saved 1.14.0. New 1.18.1. G, T, d, seed generating parameters carried coefs_obj print() summary() returned object self-describing. R Deprecated alias G, retained backward compatibility; populated value. Use G. removed future release. Use print() summary() returned object formatted display.

title: Compute True Treatment Effects — getTes
text: function internally uses auxiliary routines getNumTreats(), getP(), getFirstInds(), getTreatInds(), getActualCohortTes() determine correct indices treatment effect coefficients beta. overall treatment effect computed weighted average cohort-specific effects (uniform weights marginal DGP, propensity weights otherwise). non-marginal DGPs, \(E[\pi_g(X)]\) estimated Monte Carlo integration X distribution (Gaussian default) M = 10000 draws. Monte Carlo seed offset main coefs_obj$seed + 2L per documented seed-offset convention.

title: Compute True Treatment Effects — getTes
text: Faletto, G (2025). Fused Extended Two-Way Fixed Effects Difference--Differences Staggered Adoptions. arXiv preprint arXiv:2312.05985. https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.05985.

title: Compute True Treatment Effects — getTes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Generate coefficients coefs <- genCoefs(G = 5, T = 30, d = 12, density = 0.1, eff_size = 2, seed = 123)  # Compute the true treatment effects: te_results <- getTes(coefs)  # Overall average treatment effect on the treated: print(te_results$att_true)  # Cohort-specific treatment effects: print(te_results$actual_cohort_tes)  # True effect at each event time (periods since adoption): print(te_results$actual_event_time_tes)  # Or use the new print method for a self-describing display: print(te_results)  # Propensity-weighted truth under covariate-dependent DGP: coefs_mn <- genCoefs(G = 3, T = 5, d = 2, density = 0.5, eff_size = 2,                     assignment_type = "multinomial", assignment_strength = 1.0,                     seed = 42) te_mn <- getTes(coefs_mn) te_mn$att_true       # propensity-weighted overall ATT te_mn$cohort_weights # length G; sums to 1 } # }

what: Plot CATT or event-study estimates from a fitted BETWFE — plot.betwfe
title: Plot CATT or event-study estimates from a fitted BETWFE — plot.betwfe
text: Parallel plot.fetwfe(). BETWFE uses bridge-penalty selection mechanism, selected encoded way (TRUE = nonzero, FALSE = zeroed bridge penalty).

title: Plot CATT or event-study estimates from a fitted BETWFE — plot.betwfe
code: # S3 method for class 'betwfe' plot(x, type = c("event_study", "catt"), conf_int = TRUE, alpha = NULL, ...)

title: Plot CATT or event-study estimates from a fitted BETWFE — plot.betwfe
text: x fitted object fetwfe(), etwfe(), betwfe(). (twfeCovs currently supported – see GitHub issue #58 broader treatment twfeCovs class methods.) type Character; either "event_study" (default; event-time pooled coefficients eventStudy()) "catt" (per-cohort ATTs result$catt_df). conf_int Logical; TRUE (default), include confidence interval error bars. alpha Numeric; overrides fit's alpha CI computation. NULL (default) uses fit's alpha (95% CIs fit's alpha 0.05). default NULL, views show fit's ci_type band (simultaneous default): "catt" view reads bounds stored catt_df, "event_study" view re-derives via eventStudy() (inherits fit's ci_type). Asymmetry explicit alpha: "event_study" view, explicit alpha forwarded eventStudy(), re-runs joint machinery still produces simultaneous band alpha (fit's ci_type "simultaneous"); "catt" view, explicit alpha recomputes ci_low / ci_high estimate +/- qnorm(1 - alpha/2) * se, .e. pointwise band alpha (stored simultaneous bounds fit's alpha re-derived new alpha catt view). plot simultaneous catt bands different alpha, refit alpha call simultaneousCIs() directly. ... Currently unused; reserved future arguments.

title: Plot CATT or event-study estimates from a fitted BETWFE — plot.betwfe
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot CATT or event-study estimates from a fitted BETWFE — plot.betwfe
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   coefs <- genCoefs(G = 3, T = 6, d = 2, density = 0.5, eff_size = 2)   dat <- simulateData(coefs, N = 120, sig_eps_sq = 1, sig_eps_c_sq = 0.5, seed = 123)   res <- betwfeWithSimulatedData(dat)   if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {     plot(res)   } } # }

what: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations — simulateData
title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations — simulateData
text: Generates random panel data set simulation studies fused extended two-way fixed effects (FETWFE) estimator taking object class  "FETWFE_coefs" (produced genCoefs()) using simulate data. function creates balanced panel \(N\) units \(T\) time periods, assigns treatment status across \(G\) treated cohorts (equal marginal probabilities treatment non-treatment), constructs design matrix along corresponding outcome. covariates generated according specified distribution: default, covariates drawn normal distribution; distribution = "uniform", drawn uniformly \([-\sqrt{3}, \sqrt{3}]\). \(d = 0\) (.e. covariates), covariate-related columns interactions generated. See simulation studies section Faletto (2025) details.

title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations — simulateData
code: simulateData(   coefs_obj,   N,   sig_eps_sq,   sig_eps_c_sq,   distribution = "gaussian",   guarantee_rank_condition = FALSE,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations — simulateData
text: coefs_obj object class "FETWFE_coefs" containing coefficient vector simulation parameters. N Integer. Number units panel. sig_eps_sq Numeric. Variance idiosyncratic (observation-level) noise. sig_eps_c_sq Numeric. Variance unit-level random effects. Must non-negative; 0 allowed (yields panel unit-level random effects). distribution Character. Distribution generate covariates. Defaults "gaussian". set "uniform", covariates drawn uniformly \([-\sqrt{3}, \sqrt{3}]\). guarantee_rank_condition (Optional). Logical. TRUE, returned data set guaranteed least d + 1 units per cohort, necessary final design matrix full column rank. Default FALSE, case >= 1 unit per cohort required – permits small cohorts therefore high-dimensional (p > NT) panels, fetwfe() fits regularized regime (high-dimensional debiasedATT() path can exercised, using known data-generating coefficient vector; recovering truth requires adequate number units). seed (Optional) Controls random-number generator simulated panel. fetwfe 1.24.0 default NULL, draws ambient random-number generator (respecting preceding set.seed()) emits warning; pass integer reproducible panel, NA draw ambient generator silently. simulateData() longer reuses coefs_obj$seed (seed genCoefs() used build coefficients). Default NULL.

title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations — simulateData
text: object class "FETWFE_simulated", list containing: pdata dataframe containing generated data can passed fetwfe(). X design matrix \(X\), \(p\) columns interactions. y numeric vector length \(N \times T\) containing generated responses. covs character vector containing names generated features (\(d > 0\)), simply empty vector (\(d = 0\)) time_var name time variable pdata unit_var name unit variable pdata treatment name treatment variable pdata response name response variable pdata coefs coefficient vector \(\beta\) used data generation. first_inds vector indices indicating first treatment effect treated cohort. N_UNTREATED number never-treated units. assignments vector counts (length \(G+1\)) indicating many units fall never-treated group \(G\) treated cohorts. indep_counts Independent cohort assignments (auxiliary purposes). p number columns design matrix \(X\). N Number units. T Number time periods. G Number treated cohorts. R Deprecated alias G, retained backward compatibility; populated value. Use G. removed future release. d Number covariates. sig_eps_sq idiosyncratic noise variance. sig_eps_c_sq unit-level noise variance.

title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations — simulateData
text: function extracts simulation parameters FETWFE_coefs object passes , along additional simulation parameters, internal function simulateDataCore(). validates necessary components returned assigns S3 class "FETWFE_simulated" output. random draw controlled seed argument, coefs_obj$seed. default (seed = NULL) simulateData() draws ambient random-number generator (preceding set.seed() respected repeated calls return different panels) emits warning noting default changed fetwfe 1.24.0. Pass integer seed reproducible panel (integer always yields panel), seed = NA use ambient generator without warning. vary panel across Monte Carlo replications, pass different seed replication. Passing explicit numeric seed calls set.seed(seed) internally leaves global RNG advanced call returns. deliberate — makes simulation reproducible (seed always yields panel) varying (subsequent draws consume advanced stream). Use seed = NA (default seed = NULL) draw / preserve ambient stream without calling set.seed(). argument distribution controls generation covariates. "gaussian", covariates drawn rnorm; "uniform", drawn runif interval \([-\sqrt{3}, \sqrt{3}]\) (ensures covariates unit variance regardless distribution chosen). \(d = 0\) (.e. covariates), function omits covariate-related columns interactions.

title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations — simulateData
text: Faletto, G (2025). Fused Extended Two-Way Fixed Effects Difference--Differences Staggered Adoptions. arXiv preprint arXiv:2312.05985. https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.05985.

title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations — simulateData
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Generate coefficients   coefs <- genCoefs(G = 5, T = 30, d = 12, density = 0.1, eff_size = 2, seed = 123)    # Simulate data using the coefficients   sim_data <- simulateData(coefs, N = 120, sig_eps_sq = 5, sig_eps_c_sq = 5) } # }

what: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations (core) — simulateDataCore
title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations (core) — simulateDataCore
text: Generates random panel data set simulation studies fused extended two-way fixed effects (FETWFE) estimator. function creates balanced panel \(N\) units \(T\) time periods, assigns treatment status across \(G\) treated cohorts (equal marginal probabilities treatment non-treatment), constructs design matrix along corresponding outcome. gen_ints = TRUE full design matrix returned (including interactions covariates fixed effects treatment indicators). gen_ints = FALSE design matrix generated simpler format (interactions) expected fetwfe(). Moreover, covariates generated according specified distribution: default, covariates drawn normal distribution; distribution = "uniform", drawn uniformly \([-\sqrt{3}, \sqrt{3}]\). \(d = 0\) (.e. covariates), covariate-related columns interactions generated. See simulation studies section Faletto (2025) details.

title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations (core) — simulateDataCore
code: simulateDataCore(   N,   T,   G = NULL,   d,   sig_eps_sq,   sig_eps_c_sq,   beta,   seed = NULL,   gen_ints = FALSE,   distribution = "gaussian",   guarantee_rank_condition = FALSE,   assignment_type = "marginal",   assignment_coefs = NULL,   R = NULL )

title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations (core) — simulateDataCore
text: N Integer. Number units panel. T Integer. Number time periods. G Integer. Number treated cohorts (treatment starting periods 2 T). d Integer. Number time-invariant covariates. sig_eps_sq Numeric. Variance idiosyncratic (observation-level) noise. sig_eps_c_sq Numeric. Variance unit-level random effects. Must non-negative; 0 allowed (yields panel unit-level random effects). beta Numeric vector. Coefficient vector data generation. required length depends value gen_ints: gen_ints = TRUE d > 0, expected length \(p = G + (T-1) + d + dG + d(T-1) + num\_treats + num\_treats \times d\), \(num\_treats = T \times G - \frac{G(G+1)}{2}\). gen_ints = TRUE d = 0, expected length \(p = G + (T-1) + num\_treats\). gen_ints = FALSE, expected length \(p = G + (T-1) + d + num\_treats\). seed (Optional) Integer. Seed reproducibility. gen_ints Logical. TRUE, generate full design matrix interactions; FALSE (default), generate design matrix without interaction terms. distribution Character. Distribution generate covariates. Defaults "gaussian". set "uniform", covariates drawn uniformly \([-\sqrt{3}, \sqrt{3}]\). guarantee_rank_condition (Optional). Logical. TRUE, returned data set guaranteed least d + 1 units per cohort, necessary final design matrix full column rank. Default FALSE, case >= 1 unit per cohort required – permits small cohorts therefore high-dimensional (p > NT) panels, fetwfe() fits regularized regime (high-dimensional debiasedATT() path can exercised, using known data-generating coefficient vector; recovering truth requires adequate number units). assignment_type Character. One "marginal" (default), "multinomial", "ordered". Selects cohort-assignment DGP. "marginal" preserves pre-1.14.0 behavior. non-marginal types require non-NULL assignment_coefs argument (typically pulled FETWFE_coefs object built matching assignment_type). assignment_coefs Optional list returned .gen_assignment_coefs() (internal helper). Required assignment_type != "marginal". R Deprecated. former name G; still accepted deprecation warning, removed future release. Use G.

title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations (core) — simulateDataCore
text: object class "FETWFE_simulated", list containing: pdata dataframe containing generated data can passed fetwfe(). X design matrix. gen_ints = TRUE, \(X\) \(p\) columns interactions; gen_ints = FALSE, \(X\) interactions. y numeric vector length \(N \times T\) containing generated responses. covs character vector containing names generated features (\(d > 0\)), simply empty vector (\(d = 0\)) time_var name time variable pdata unit_var name unit variable pdata treatment name treatment variable pdata response name response variable pdata coefs coefficient vector \(\beta\) used data generation. first_inds vector indices indicating first treatment effect treated cohort. N_UNTREATED number never-treated units. assignments vector counts (length \(G+1\)) indicating many units fall never-treated group \(G\) treated cohorts. indep_counts Independent cohort assignments (auxiliary purposes). p number columns design matrix \(X\). N Number units. T Number time periods. G Number treated cohorts. R Deprecated alias G, retained backward compatibility; populated value. Use G. removed future release. d Number covariates. sig_eps_sq idiosyncratic noise variance. sig_eps_c_sq unit-level noise variance.

title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations (core) — simulateDataCore
text: gen_ints = TRUE, function constructs design matrix first generating base fixed effects long-format covariate matrix (via generateBaseEffects()), appending interactions covariates cohort/time fixed effects (via generateFEInts()) finally treatment indicator columns treatment-covariate interactions (via genTreatVarsSim() genTreatInts()). gen_ints = FALSE, design matrix consists base fixed effects, covariates, treatment indicators. argument distribution controls generation covariates. "gaussian", covariates drawn rnorm; "uniform", drawn runif interval \([-\sqrt{3}, \sqrt{3}]\). \(d = 0\) (.e. covariates), function omits covariate-related columns interactions.

title: Generate Random Panel Data for FETWFE Simulations (core) — simulateDataCore
text: Faletto, G (2025). Fused Extended Two-Way Fixed Effects Difference--Differences Staggered Adoptions. arXiv preprint arXiv:2312.05985. https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.05985.

what: Parametric simultaneous (1 - alpha) confidence intervals over a family of treatment effects — simultaneousCIs
title: Parametric simultaneous (1 - alpha) confidence intervals over a family of treatment effects — simultaneousCIs
text: Computes simultaneous (family-wise) confidence intervals user- specified family treatment effects fitted FETWFE / ETWFE / BETWFE / twfeCovs object. simultaneous critical value c_{1 - alpha} (1 - alpha) quantile max_k |Z_k| Z follows multivariate normal correlation matrix cov2cor(Sigma); computed deterministically via mvtnorm::qmvnorm(). Faletto (2025) Theorem (c') tight Gaussianity Assumption (Psi-), family psi-linear effects asymptotically multivariate normal covariance estimable package's existing variance machinery; paper's fixed-dim framing high-dimensional correction needed. pointwise critical value qnorm(1 - alpha/2) (per-effect coverage) Bonferroni-conservative critical value qnorm(1 - alpha/(2K)) (family- wise coverage correlation assumption) returned side--side comparison; simultaneous critical value always effects positively correlated (typical difference-- differences, effects share regression-coefficient variance piece).

title: Parametric simultaneous (1 - alpha) confidence intervals over a family of treatment effects — simultaneousCIs
code: simultaneousCIs(   result,   family = c("event_study", "cohort", "all_post_treatment", "custom"),   alpha = NULL,   contrasts = NULL,   method = c("analytic", "bootstrap"),   B = 1000L,   seed = NULL,   multiplier = c("rademacher", "mammen", "webb"),   lambda_c = 1,   riesz_max_iter = 5000L,   riesz_tol = 1e-09 )

title: Parametric simultaneous (1 - alpha) confidence intervals over a family of treatment effects — simultaneousCIs
text: result fitted object class "fetwfe", "etwfe", "betwfe", "twfeCovs". family Character; one "event_study", "cohort", "all_post_treatment", "custom". See Details family's resolution. alpha Numeric (0, 1); significance level. Default NULL, inherits fit's alpha (level catt_df / displayed simultaneous bands built ), simultaneousCIs(fit) reproduces fit's shown simultaneous bounds. Pass explicit value override. contrasts family = "custom", K x num_treats matrix whose rows give K linear combinations underlying per-(g, t) treatment-effect vector (multcomp::glht() convention; num_treats number estimated effects, equal G twfeCovs). Ignored families. Note "custom" family omits cohort-probability variance term (Sigma_2 = 0), custom contrast pools across cohorts probability-weighted way anti-conservative (band can -cover); use family = "cohort" cohort-pooled effects. method Character; simultaneous critical value computed. "analytic" (default) uses exact multivariate-normal sup-t quantile via mvtnorm::qmvnorm(). "bootstrap" uses multiplier bootstrap Chernozhukov, Chetverikov & Kato (2013): perturbs per-unit influence functions reads sup-t critical value bootstrap distribution. two asymptotically equivalent; bootstrap scales better large effect families heteroskedasticity/cluster-robust construction (always reports cluster-robust per-unit standard errors, regardless fit's se_type). method = "bootstrap" supports four families. family = "event_study" bootstrap additionally perturbs per-unit cohort-probability (propensity) influence function independent multiplier stream – two-channel bootstrap matching analytic variance Sigma = Sigma_1 + Sigma_2 (event-study family one whose Sigma_2 non-zero, event-time effect pools across cohorts weighted estimated cohort probabilities). (full) design high-dimensional (p >= NT) – analytic Gram inverse need exist – bootstrap uses full-design desparsified construction debiasedATT() (per-effect nodewise directions) generalized family. desparsified p >= NT band path (fetwfe() fits ) generalizes debiasedATT() construction — whose overall-ATT coverage validated near-nominally simulation (Theorem debiased.highdim.thm, Faletto 2025) — family functionals. family-wise coverage likewise validated near-nominally (Theorem debiased.highdim.joint.thm): ~0.92 event-study family (K = 7) p >= NT anchor Faletto (2025), band sibling scalar result (~0.94); cohort -post-treatment families covered theorem separately simulated. scalar, validity rests sparsity / restricted-eigenvalue primitives (assumed, proved), inspect returned feasibility / converged diagnostics. non-fetwfe() p >= NT fit (e.g. betwfe()) desparsified band, instead falls back fixed-p selected-support band emits warning(): #308 coverage study shows band substantially -covers p >= NT regime – even selected support low-dimensional – bridge shrinks band center toward zero post-selection standard error understates variability, band returned treated unreliable (use fetwfe() fit valid high-dimensional bands). desparsified path covers four families (high-dimensional family = "event_study" fit additionally carries propensity channel F_pi). high-dimensional regime band centered debiased estimate (high-dimensional FETWFE theory correction, equal debiasedATT()'s point estimate matching contrast), post-selection bridge estimate; fixed-p bands center (unbiased) bridge estimate . desparsified bootstrap band built empirical per-unit influence function – needing neither GLS whitening sig_eps_sq – also accepts high-dimensional gls = FALSE fetwfe fit (calc_ses = FALSE), band analog debiasedATT()'s Omega-free SE (#307, #313). method = "analytic" still requires valid analytic SEs (q < 1, gls = TRUE, rank-satisfied fit). p >= NT, method = "bootstrap" valid simultaneous band — desparsified Theorem debiased.highdim.joint.thm band . method = "analytic" call instead returns analytic band selected support: post-selection band -covers (uniformly-valid desparsified band), gls = FALSE fit analytic SEs — pass method = "bootstrap" high-dimensional bands. B Integer; number multiplier-bootstrap replicates (method = "bootstrap" ). Default 1000. seed Optional integer; supplied, bootstrap draws reproducible (ambient random-number stream saved restored around ). NULL (default), draws come ambient generator, results vary run run. Ignored method = "analytic". multiplier Character; multiplier-weight distribution bootstrap: "rademacher" (default, +/-1), "mammen" (Mammen 1993 two-point distribution), "webb" (Webb 2013 six-point distribution, preferred clusters). Ignored method = "analytic". lambda_c, riesz_max_iter, riesz_tol Controls high-dimensional (p >= NT) bootstrap, effect's debiasing direction nodewise (desparsified-lasso) riesz_lasso() solve. lambda_c leading constant penalty lambda_node = lambda_c * max(||) * sqrt(log p / N): either single positive number (default 1.0 = theory scale) string "cv", selects constant per fit cross-validation (CV debiasedATT() uses, overall-ATT direction, one constant serves point estimate every band effect; #295). Smaller fixed values can leave directions infeasible (warning fires bands unreliable). Ignored p < NT method = "analytic". default stays fixed 1.0; family-wise coverage validated Faletto (2025) used "cv" selector (lambda_c ~ 0.54, feasibility 1.0), constant serves scalar debiasedATT() every band effect.

title: Parametric simultaneous (1 - alpha) confidence intervals over a family of treatment effects — simultaneousCIs
text: object S3 class "simultaneous_cis": list ci data frame columns effect, estimate, simultaneous_ci_low, simultaneous_ci_high, pointwise_ci_low, pointwise_ci_high (one row per effect family). adjusted_p_values Numeric vector length K: single-step max-T multiplicity-adjusted (family-wise) p-value effect, exact dual simultaneous band (coefficient lies outside (1 - alpha) band iff adjusted p-value < alpha). Computed via mvtnorm::pmvnorm() correlation matrix band uses (, se_type = "conservative", Bonferroni adjustment min(1, K * pointwise_p)). NA degenerate (zero-variance) effects. (#200) critical_value simultaneous critical value c_{1 - alpha} (, fit used se_type = "conservative", Bonferroni critical value qnorm(1 - alpha/(2K)) – see Details). pointwise_critical_value qnorm(1 - alpha/2), reference. bonferroni_critical_value qnorm(1 - alpha/(2K)), reference. family requested family (character). alpha significance level used. K number effects family (integer). method = "bootstrap" list additionally carries method, B, seed, multiplier, regime ("fixed-p" "high-dimensional"); critical_value bootstrap sup-t quantile standard errors backing ci cluster-robust per-unit ones (non-cluster fit bootstrap band may differ analytic homoskedastic band). "high-dimensional" fit additionally carries per-effect feasibility, converged, lambda_node (nodewise-direction diagnostics), plus lambda_c (leading constant used) lambda_c_selection ("fixed" "cv").

title: Parametric simultaneous (1 - alpha) confidence intervals over a family of treatment effects — simultaneousCIs
text: Family resolution K. "event_study" resolves one effect per post-treatment event time e = 0, ..., T - 2 (K = T - 1); "cohort" one effect per treated cohort (K = G); "all_post_treatment" one effect per (g, t) cell (K = num_treats); "custom" K = nrow(contrasts) user-supplied contrasts. Joint covariance. K x K covariance Sigma = Sigma_1 + Sigma_2 reconstructed call time fit's stored slots (design matrix, selected support, theta_hat / beta_hat, cohort_probs_overall, sig_eps_sq). Sigma_1 regression-coefficient piece Sigma_2 cohort-probability piece, generalizing package's per-point variance machinery (machinery eventStudy() uses). construction sqrt(diag(Sigma)) equals package's existing per-point standard errors corresponding effects. Sigma blocks persisted fit; re-derivation sub-second. Degenerate (zero-variance) effects. effect whose entire contribution selected support zeroed bridge penalty – , scattered-cohort panels, event time empty valid-cohort set – standard error exactly 0 construction, simultaneous pointwise CIs collapse point estimate excluded joint correlation matrix (adds family-wise risk; critical value computed non-degenerate sub-family). se = 0 convention simultaneous-CI analog NA standard error eventStudy() reports structurally-degenerate event times; assign effect estimate 0. Paper grounding. Theorem (c') tight Gaussianity (Faletto 2025, paper_arxiv.tex:1233) guarantees joint asymptotic normality; Assumption (Psi-) (assumption equation paper_arxiv.tex:2013; -prose discussion paper line 1268) influence-function condition package's default cohort-sample-proportions estimator satisfies; fixed-dim framing follows paper's AE point 1(d). Conservative fallback. fit made se_type = "conservative", function falls back Bonferroni-corrected pointwise CIs (Cauchy-Schwarz upper bound used conservative scalar SE generalize K x K covariance matrix) emits brief message(). $critical_value field set Bonferroni value branch. Numerical integration. critical value computed via mvtnorm::qmvnorm(..., algorithm = mvtnorm::GenzBretz()) (mvtnorm's default quasi-Monte Carlo integrator; sub-second K 100, K cap practical concern FETWFE families). mvtnorm Imports dependency (version 1.16.0, simultaneous bands became default reported confidence interval; see ci_type argument fetwfe()). function uses K > 1 se_type != "conservative" (K = 1 conservative paths bypass dependency), retains defensive stop() actionable message somehow unavailable (e.g., corrupted install). Determinism contract. function deterministic inputs: fit plus family, alpha, contrasts always produces critical value across calls. achieved wrapping internal mvtnorm::qmvnorm() call save/restore caller's .Random.seed fixed internal set.seed(1L) immediately call. function mutate caller's .Random.seed (save/restore via .exit() leaves caller's RNG state identical pre- post-call), matching convention adopted R/fetwfe_core.R::getBetaCV() PR #181 / v1.13.5. Users need call set.seed() simultaneousCIs() get reproducible results, downstream RNG-using code observes perturbation.

title: Parametric simultaneous (1 - alpha) confidence intervals over a family of treatment effects — simultaneousCIs
text: Faletto, G. (2025). Fused Extended Two-Way Fixed Effects Difference-- Differences Staggered Adoptions. arXiv:2312.05985. Hothorn, T., Bretz, F., & Westfall, P. (2008). Simultaneous Inference General Parametric Models. Biometrical Journal 50(3), 346-363.

title: Parametric simultaneous (1 - alpha) confidence intervals over a family of treatment effects — simultaneousCIs
code: # \donttest{   coefs <- genCoefs(G = 3, T = 6, d = 2, density = 0.5, eff_size = 2)   sim <- simulateData(coefs, N = 120, sig_eps_sq = 1, sig_eps_c_sq = 0.5, seed = 123)   fit <- fetwfeWithSimulatedData(sim)   sci <- simultaneousCIs(fit, family = "event_study", alpha = 0.05)   print(sci) #> Parametric simultaneous (1 - alpha) confidence intervals #> Family: event_study, K = 5, alpha = 0.05 #> Simultaneous critical value: 2.4454 (pointwise 1.9600, Bonferroni 2.5758) #>  #>  effect estimate simultaneous_ci_low simultaneous_ci_high pointwise_ci_low #>      e0 0.000000           0.0000000             0.000000         0.000000 #>      e1 1.354096           0.9933308             1.714862         1.064944 #>      e2 3.242404           2.8548785             3.629930         2.931804 #>      e3 3.700280           2.9969740             4.403586         3.136583 #>      e4 5.751528           5.3111327             6.191922         5.398553 #>  pointwise_ci_high #>           0.000000 #>           1.643248 #>           3.553005 #>           4.263977 #>           6.104502 # }

Carries styled print / summary / coef surface three sibling estimators, plus tidy / glance (broom) simultaneousCIs. plot augment intentionally provided: twfeCovs() estimates one pooled effect per cohort, per-(cohort, time) / event-study basis plot, coefficient vector reduced basis augment()'s fitted-value path match (#58). raise informative error.

title: Two-way fixed effects with covariates and a single pooled treatment effect per cohort — twfeCovs
text: WARNING: function used estimation. biased estimator treatment effects. Implementation two-way fixed effects covariates single pooled treatment effect per cohort. Estimates overall ATT well CATT (cohort average treatment effects treated units). implemented sake simulation studies Faletto (2025). estimator unbiased assumptions treatment effects homogeneous across covariates identical within cohorts across times since treatment.

title: Two-way fixed effects with covariates and a single pooled treatment effect per cohort — twfeCovs
code: twfeCovs(   pdata,   time_var,   unit_var,   treatment,   response,   covs = c(),   indep_counts = NA,   sig_eps_sq = NA,   sig_eps_c_sq = NA,   verbose = FALSE,   alpha = 0.05,   add_ridge = FALSE,   allow_no_never_treated = TRUE,   se_type = "default",   ci_type = c("simultaneous", "pointwise") )

title: Two-way fixed effects with covariates and a single pooled treatment effect per cohort — twfeCovs
text: pdata Dataframe; panel data set. row represent observation unit time. contain columns described . time_var Character; name single column containing variable time period. column expected contain integer values (example, years). Recommended encodings dates include format YYYY, YYYYMM, YYYYMMDD, whichever appropriate data. unit_var Character; name single column containing variable unit. column expected contain character values (.e. "name" unit). treatment Character; name single column containing variable treatment dummy indicator. column expected contain integer values, particular, equal 0 unit untreated time 1 otherwise. Treatment absorbing state; , unit treated time t, must also treated times t + 1, ..., T. units treated first time period removed automatically. Please make sure least units remain untreated final time period ("never-treated units"). response Character; name single column containing response unit time. response must integer numeric value. covs (Optional.) Either character vector containing names columns covariates (e.g., covs = c("x1", "x2")), one-sided formula (e.g., covs = ~ x1 + x2) – formula form mirrors convention used ::att_gt(xformla = ...). additive bare variable names supported formula form; derived variables, compute data frame first pass via character-vector form. columns expected contain integer, numeric, factor values, categorical values automatically encoded binary indicators. covariates provided, treatment effect estimation proceed, valid unconditional versions parallel trends anticipation assumptions. Default c(). indep_counts (Optional.) Integer; vector. sufficiently large number units, can optionally randomly split data set half (N units data set). data half units go pdata argument provided . N units, simply provide counts many units appear untreated cohort plus G cohorts argument indep_counts. benefit standard error average treatment effect (asymptotically) exact instead conservative. length indep_counts must equal 1 plus number treated cohorts pdata. entries indep_counts must strictly positive (concerned might work , maybe data set small side best just leave full data set pdata). sum counts indep_counts must match total number units pdata. Default NA (case conservative standard error formula used). sig_eps_sq (Optional.) Numeric; variance row-level IID noise assumed apply observation. See Section 2 Faletto (2025) details. best provide variance known (example, using simulated data). variance unknown, argument can omitted, variance estimated REML linear mixed-effects model y ~ X + (1 | unit) via lme4::lmer (Bates et al. 2015; Patterson & Thompson 1971). Default NA. sig_eps_c_sq (Optional.) Numeric; variance unit-level IID noise (random effects) assumed apply observation. See Section 2 Faletto (2025) details. best provide variance known (example, using simulated data). variance unknown, argument can omitted, variance estimated REML via lme4::lmer linear mixed-effects model y ~ X + (1 | unit) (Bates et al. 2015; Patterson & Thompson 1971). Default NA. verbose Logical; TRUE, details progress function printed function executes. Default FALSE. alpha Numeric; function calculate (1 - alpha) confidence intervals cohort average treatment effects returned catt_df. add_ridge (Optional.) Logical; TRUE, adds small amount ridge regularization (untransformed) coefficients stabilize estimation. Default FALSE. allow_no_never_treated (Optional.) Logical; TRUE (default) input panel contains never-treated units, panel auto-truncated dropping time periods latest cohort's start time — units latest cohort serve never-treated comparison group retained sub-panel — warning naming dropped periods. FALSE, estimator stops error case (package's behavior prior version 1.5.6). argument effect input already contains never-treated units. Default TRUE. se_type Character; one "default", "conservative", "cluster". "default" returns tight Gaussian variance sqrt(att_var_1 + att_var_2) Theorem (c$'$) Assumption (Psi-) (asymptotically exact package's default cohort sample-proportions estimator); "conservative" returns Cauchy-Schwarz upper bound Theorem (c) (use propensity-score estimator violates (Psi-)); "cluster" experimental unit-clustered Liang-Zeger sandwich SE OLS-selected support (see companion vignette inference_vignette formula, assumptions, theory-pending caveat). Default "default". v1.12.0 introduced tight Gaussian default; versions <= 1.11.7 used conservative Cauchy-Schwarz formula default. ci_type Character; one "simultaneous" (default) "pointwise". Controls confidence-interval bounds reported cohort-specific ATTs (catt_df). "simultaneous" reports parametric simultaneous (family-wise, uniform) bands computed via simultaneousCIs(): band covers cohort effects jointly probability 1 - alpha, matching default presentation ::aggte(cband = TRUE). "pointwise" reports per-effect Wald intervals (covers effect probability 1 - alpha, joint guarantee — behavior versions <= 1.15.1). interval bounds per-cohort p-values (p_value) follow ci_type (single-step max-T multiplicity-adjusted "simultaneous", per-cohort Wald "pointwise"; #200); standard errors (se) identical settings. twfeCovs estimates single pooled effect per cohort, cohort family affected (event-study surface). standard errors unavailable (e.g., rank-deficient design) bounds NA settings. Default "simultaneous".

title: Two-way fixed effects with covariates and a single pooled treatment effect per cohort — twfeCovs
text: object class twfeCovs containing following elements: att_hat estimated overall average treatment effect randomly selected treated unit. att_se standard error ATT. Gram matrix invertible, NA. att_p_value two-sided p-value overall ATT null H_0: tau = 0, computed 2 * pnorm(-|att_hat / att_se|). NA att_se zero NA. Standard post-OLS interpretation; twfeCovs perform selection. catt_hats named vector containing estimated average treatment effects cohort. catt_ses named vector containing (asymptotically exact) standard errors estimated average treatment effects within cohort. cohort_probs vector estimated probabilities cohort conditional treated, used calculating att_hat. indep_counts provided, cohort_probs calculated ; otherwise, calculated counts units treated cohort pdata. catt_df data frame (S3 class c("catt_df", "data.frame")) displaying cohort names (cohort), average treatment effects (estimate), standard errors (se), 1 - alpha confidence interval bounds (ci_low, ci_high), per-cohort p-values (p_value). selected column; twfeCovs perform selection. catt_df S3 class makes [[ / $ / [ access pre-1.11.0 Title-Case column names (Cohort, Estimated TE, SE, ConfIntLow, ConfIntHigh, P_value) stop() migration message pointing new name. See NEWS.md rename table. beta_hat full vector estimated coefficients. treat_inds indices beta_hat corresponding treatment effects cohort. treat_int_inds indices beta_hat corresponding interactions treatment effects cohort covariates. sig_eps_sq Either provided sig_eps_sq estimated one, value provided. sig_eps_c_sq Either provided sig_eps_c_sq estimated one, value provided. X_ints design matrix created containing interactions, time cohort dummies, etc. y vector responses, containing nrow(X_ints) entries. X_final design matrix applying change coordinates fit model also multiplying left square root inverse estimated covariance matrix unit. y_final final response multiplying left square root inverse estimated covariance matrix unit. N final number units  data set used estimation (units may removed treated first time period). T number time periods final data set. G final number treated cohorts appear final data set. R Deprecated alias G, retained backward compatibility; populated value. Use G. removed future release. d final number covariates appear final data set (covariates may removed contained missing values contained value every unit). p final number columns full set covariates used estimate model. y_mean Numeric scalar; mean original (pre-centering) response. Stored downstream methods (augment(), predict()) can return fitted values original-response scale. response_col_name Character scalar; response column name original pdata. Reserved future augment() / predict() methods. time_var, unit_var, treatment Character scalars; corresponding arguments user passed. covs Character vector; original covs argument (pre-factor- expansion). calc_ses Logical indicating whether standard errors calculated. cohort_probs_overall vector estimated cohort probabilities overall sample (treated untreated), used computing variance overall ATT. indep_counts_used Logical scalar; TRUE valid indep_counts argument provided used asymptotically-exact ATT inference, FALSE otherwise. se_type Character scalar; se_type argument user passed ("default", "conservative", "cluster"). alpha alpha level used confidence intervals. ci_type Character scalar; ci_type argument user passed ("simultaneous" "pointwise"), controlling whether reported catt_df confidence-interval bounds simultaneous (family-wise) pointwise. internal list containing internal outputs typically needed interpretation, packaged parity fetwfe() downstream consumers can use single canonical access path across four estimator classes (#144). first five sub-slots (X_ints, y, X_final, y_final, calc_ses) also duplicated top level backward compat; variance_components first_year live $internal: X_ints design matrix containing interactions, time cohort dummies, etc. value top-level X_ints. y vector responses. top-level y. X_final design matrix change--coordinates step. top-level X_final. y_final transformed response vector. top-level y_final. calc_ses Logical indicating whether standard errors calculated. top-level calc_ses. variance_components list exposing two variance pieces (att_var_1, att_var_2) plus paper-notation counterparts (V_1, V_2) unit-scaled variance estimators (tilde_v_N, hat_v_N, tilde_v_N_C, tilde_v_N_C_pi_hat, tilde_v_N_C_pi_hat_cons, tilde_v_N_cons). Wald CI [hat_T_N +- qnorm(1-alpha/2) * sqrt(tilde_v_N / N)] (paper Eq. conf.int.form). New v1.12.0 (issue #141 + #146). first_year Integer numeric scalar; first (earliest) time_var value panel idCohorts() processing. Consumed eventStudy() map cohort_probs' cohort labels (treatment-start years) 1-based panel-time-index offsets labels integer-coercible. New v1.13.3 (issue #174).

Bates, D., Maechler, M., Bolker, B., & Walker, S. (2015). Fitting Linear Mixed-Effects Models Using lme4. Journal Statistical Software, 67(1), 1-48. doi:10.18637/jss.v067.i01 . Patterson, H. title: Two-way fixed effects with covariates and a single pooled treatment effect per cohort — twfeCovs
text: Gregory Faletto

title: Two-way fixed effects with covariates and a single pooled treatment effect per cohort — twfeCovs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(bacondecomp)  data(castle)  # Response: the log homicide rate. Treatment: `cdl` records the share of # the year the castle-doctrine law was in effect, so `cdl > 0` gives the # absorbing 0/1 treatment indicator. castle$l_homicide <- log(castle$homicide) castle$treated <- as.integer(castle$cdl > 0)  # No `covs` here: twfeCovs is pure OLS (no bridge penalty), and castle's # smallest adoption cohorts contain a single state, so the design is # rank-deficient once any covariate is added. res <- twfeCovs(     pdata = castle,     time_var = "year",     unit_var = "state",     treatment = "treated",     response = "l_homicide",     verbose = TRUE)  # Print results print(res, max_cohorts = Inf) } # }

title: Run twfeCovs on Simulated Data — twfeCovsWithSimulatedData
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Generate coefficients   coefs <- genCoefs(G = 5, T = 30, d = 12, density = 0.1, eff_size = 2, seed = 123)    # Simulate data using the coefficients   sim_data <- simulateData(coefs, N = 120, sig_eps_sq = 5, sig_eps_c_sq = 5, seed = 123)    result <- twfeCovsWithSimulatedData(sim_data) } # }
